Repo-wide grep for cvss_vector/affected_fixed/per-distro/disclosure-template/mermaid in mcp+prompts+.claude+docs+scripts returned nothing report-side.

*Recommendation:* Real but narrow gap, and the candidate is over-scoped for Bob's model. Bob runs an active GHSA/CVE disclosure campaign (netatalk, exiv2, openexr, FreeRDP, AeroSpace, stable-diffusion.cpp, etc.) and the operator hand-transcribes report.md into advisory web forms, so cutting that friction has recurring value. The transferable subset: (1) a CWE line on OSS findings — a genuine inconsistency, since web and smart-contract branches already emit CWE but the OSS branch does not; (2) a suggested CVSS vector string assembled from the data Bob already computes (severity + attack_vector AV:N/AV:L) — clearly labeled 'suggested' so the operator can adjust; (3) a references list (parent residual-hunt CVE/GHSA Bob already mines, the CWE link, an upstream code permalink). REJECT the poc-lab-specific pieces: the per-distro affected/fixed matrix and fix_commit are CVE-REDISCOVERY-shaped (poc-lab rediscovers already-patched distro CVEs), which is the inverse of Bob's 'new 0-day in upstream at the scanned commit' model — there is no fix commit at disclosure time and usually one affected version. The disclosure timeline lives in the operator's life-context memory, not the session, so a timeline section would render mostly empty at compose time. Mermaid is cosmetic and GHSA rendering of it is unreliable; Bob already has chain_summary. Net: adopt CWE+suggested-CVSS+references for the OSS branch only; skip matrix/fix_commit/timeline/mermaid.

*Integration:* Lowest-effort, lowest-risk path is PROMPT-ONLY (true 'S'), not a schema change. The existing free-prose sections already accept this: instruct the OSS branch of prompts/roles/reporter.md (lines 28-34) and the mirror in .claude/agents/report-writer.md (lines 40-44) to include, inside the existing 'evidence'/'severity'/'remediation' section prose, a 'Disclosure metadata' block for capability_pack starting with 'oss_': CWE id+name, a suggested CVSS-4.0 vector string derived from final-verifier severity + the surface's attack_vector (AV:N when network_reachable, AV:L otherwise — Bob already carries these), affected version/commit (the scanned ref), and references (residual-hunt parent CVE/GHSA from residual_hunt_targets, CWE URL, upstream permalink). This needs ZERO change to compose-report.js and avoids the registry/test churn that CLAUDE.md flags (SECTION_KINDS feeds the closed inputSchema enum + generated surfaces + test:mcp's 1600+ assertions). If a structured slot is later wanted, add one new section kind (e.g. 'disclosure_metadata') to SECTION_KINDS in mcp/lib/tools/compose-report.js:49-57 plus its renderMarkdown branch and the inputSchema enum, then re-run node scripts/generate-agent-tools.js and npm run test:mcp — but that is M effort, not S, and the prose path already delivers the value. Gate the whole block on capability_pack.startsWith('oss_') so the lean web/SC triage templates are untouched.

#### patched-unpatched-differential-oracle — Patched-vs-unpatched differential repro oracle  
**verdict:** adapt-partial · already-covered: false · effort M / value high

*Bob evidence:* Bob's LIVE pipeline has no patched-vs-unpatched code differential. (1) The exact "vul=exit1/fix=exit0" oracle exists ONLY as benchmark SCORING methodology in docs/BOB_OSS_BENCHMARK_PLAN.md (lines 126, 205, 383), relying on ARVO already supplying fix_commit; never run during Bob's own discovery/verification. (2) OSS sessions are single-commit: bob_init_repo_session takes one commit (init-repo-session.js:44-46) pinned as repo_hash so the docker image tag stays stable (repo-target.js:170); a second/fixed-commit build breaks that stable-tag assumption. (3) All three verifiers replay the native finding on that single pinned commit via bob_repo_check/bob_repo_docker_run (oss_native_code -> bob_repo_check/repo_native_code_check in verifier tables of .claude/agents/{brutalist,balanced,final}-verifier.md); no verifier builds a fixed/clean commit. (4) Only live code differentials are HTTP auth-state (run-auth-differential.js, C4) and SC fresh-state replay (omit fork_block); neither is a code-version differential. (5) Evidence-pack schema (write-evidence-packs.js) has replay_summary plus per-finding evidence refs but NO differential/two-build row, and grader proof_quality (0-25, grader.md:19) gives no credit for a fires-on-vuln/clean-on-fixed differential. (6) The one live fix_commit reference (bob-spec.js:173) is ingested-audit-issue provenance metadata, never a build target.

*Recommendation:* Real gap, genuinely valuable, but narrower than the candidate frames it, hence adapt-partial not full adopt. The literal "build the fixing commit" only applies when a fix already exists; for a cold 0-day on HEAD there is no fix commit to build. Where it pays off is exactly Bob's bread-and-butter OSS output: (a) incomplete-fix/residual findings (netatalk residual of CVE-2026-44066, rpcbind residual of Steve's 2018 fix, libraw residual of the 2020 commit) where a differential row proving "the upstream fix-commit IS present in my build yet the bug STILL fires" directly rebuts the #1 maintainer pushback Bob keeps hitting ("this was already fixed" — openexr/libde265) and maps to the hunting rule that "audit reports this fixed" is not a stop condition; and (b) self-patch validation ("fires on HEAD, clean with my proposed patch") which strengthens patch-offered disclosures (libraw, libredwg). Costs are non-trivial: a second build doubles the most failure-prone arch-sensitive step (memory is full of aarch64 ASAN/compiler-rt/libFuzzer build pain) and fights the single-commit/stable-image-tag session model. Adopt the differential IDEA, reframed and optional, gated to high/critical oss_native_code; reject the secret-dump execution (already excluded and barred by the PII rule).

*Integration:* Concrete touch points: (1) Reframe the control side into three kinds rather than "the fixing commit": upstream_fix (rebuild at a ref containing the claimed-existing fix; residual/incomplete-fix findings), self_patch (git apply Bob's proposed patch on HEAD), pre_introduction (rebuild before the regressing commit; cold-0day regression proof). (2) Run as an OPTIONAL verifier step reusing the EXISTING bob_repo_docker_run writable path: a compose-role command that stages /src into /work/repo, runs git checkout <ref> or git apply <patch>, rebuilds, reruns the same repro; record both run_ids. No new runner needed and it sidesteps the single-commit session-pin problem because the second build happens inside the container, not via a second bob_init_repo_session. (3) Add a differential evidence row to the pack schema in mcp/lib/tools/write-evidence-packs.js + mcp/lib/evidence.js: { vuln_run_id, vuln_exit_code, control_run_id, control_exit_code, control_kind, verdict } so the fires/clean pair is first-class and hash-bound. (4) Add the optional OSS-repo differential step to .claude/agents/{brutalist,balanced,final}-verifier.md + prompts/roles/{brutalist,balanced,final}-verifier.md, gated to severity in {high,critical} AND capability_pack oss_native_code. (5) Small grader nudge: proof_quality bonus when a differential row is present (.claude/agents/grader.md + prompts/roles/grader.md). (6) Keep it x86_64-preferred and optional given documented aarch64 build friction; the doubled build is the main cost so do not make it a gate. Regenerate surfaces (node scripts/generate-agent-tools.js) if the evidence schema shifts role bundles, and add tests under test/. Effort is M for the residual/self-patch happy path, closer to M-L once evidence-row + grader + 4 verifier prompts + tests are wired.

#### exploitation-completeness-label — Structured exploitation-completeness label on findings  
**verdict:** already-covered · already-covered: true · effort S / value low

*Bob evidence:* The trigger-vs-weaponized distinction is already machine-legible in Bob, distributed across three authoritative structured surfaces that the grader's severity-accuracy axis consumes: (1) `mcp/lib/tools/write-verification-round.js` encodes per-finding `disposition` (confirmed/denied/downgraded), a re-assessed authoritative `severity`, a `reportable` boolean, and `confidence`+`confidence_reasons` enums (`manual_inference`, `agreement_not_replayed`, `tooling_blocked`, `state_changed`) that capture exactly when a claim is a bare trigger vs a replayed full exploit. (2) `mcp/lib/tools/write-chain-attempt.js` has an `outcome` enum (confirmed/denied/blocked/inconclusive/not_applicable) and the grader's `chain_potential` axis (grader.md line 21) scores weaponization/chaining — a trigger that was never chained into impact scores low here. (3) `.claude/agents/balanced-verifier.md:54` documents precondition-gated severity ("HIGH if an attacker can trigger the upgrade; LOW if the path is admin-only"), the exact non-universal/preconditions honesty the candidate cites. The grader (grader.md lines 19-21) already scores `severity_accuracy` (0-15) and `proof_quality` (0-25, 'Is the PoC complete, reproducible...') off these structured inputs. The only thing absent is a single named `exploitation_completeness` enum field; `bob_record_candidate_claim` (record-candidate-claim.js) carries `severity`, `validated` bool, `proof_of_concept`, `impact`, `sc_evidence` re-run handle but no such enum.

*Recommendation:* Reject as a redundant structuring delta. The candidate's own framing ("Bob already enforces honest severity-gating, so this is a small structuring delta, not a new capability") concedes the point, and investigation confirms the capability is fully covered: the trigger-vs-weaponized signal already flows through the final-verifier's authoritative severity reassessment + reportable + confidence_reasons, the chain-attempt outcome enum + chain_potential grader axis, and documented verifier precondition-gating rules — all of which already feed severity_accuracy. Adding a named enum gives the grader-LLM no information it doesn't already have. Worse, the proposed integration point (bob_record_candidate_claim, hunter-self-reported at record time, pre-verification) actively fights Bob's freeze-then-verify design, whose whole premise is that the hunter's self-assessment is untrustworthy and the final-verifier is authoritative on severity (report-writer.md: 'final-verifier severity is authoritative'). A hunter-asserted completeness label re-introduces exactly the optimistic self-grading bias the architecture exists to suppress. If a consolidated label were ever genuinely wanted (e.g. for cleaner report headers), the only defensible layer is the verification round (verifier-asserted, post-replay), not the candidate claim — but even there it would be derivable from existing fields, so the ROI does not justify schema churn across the claim contract, verification contract, grader rubric, and their snapshot/hash-bound tests.

*Integration:* No integration recommended. If revisited despite the low ROI: do NOT add the field to bob_record_candidate_claim/record-candidate-claim.js (hunter self-report, pre-verification, conflicts with freeze-then-verify). The only coherent placement is a verifier-asserted optional enum on write-verification-round.js results[] (alongside disposition/confidence_reasons), derived during final verification, then surfaced as an input hint to grader.md's severity_accuracy axis. That would still be largely redundant with disposition + confidence_reasons + chain outcome, and would require touching the verification contract, snapshot/hash-bound JSONL ledgers, and the frozen test fixtures (test/verification-contracts.js, grade-from-frozen-payload.test.js), which is disproportionate to the marginal benefit.


---

## Capability profiles


### Profile: bob

## Identity (one paragraph: what this project IS, plainly)

Hacker Bob is a **portable, registry-driven MCP framework for autonomous-but-human-gated security evaluation** — bug-bounty web pentests, multi-chain smart-contract audits, and open-source source audits — packaged as a Claude Code skill (`/bob-evaluate`) plus three sibling host adapters (Codex, Kimi, generic MCP). Concretely it is a single Node.js stdio MCP server (`mcp/server.js`, Node ≥20, version 2.0.0, Apache-2.0) exposing ~148 frozen security tools, fronted by a thin LLM "orchestrator" agent that owns *no durable state of its own*: all orchestration truth — sessions, waves, an event-sourced attack-surface frontier, a task-graph, frozen claim batches, adversarial verification rounds, grades, and chains — lives server-side as hash-bound, lock-serialized JSON/JSONL on disk under `~/hacker-bob-sessions/<target>/`. The animating thesis is that the system's edge comes from **MCP-owned, graph-shaped, content-addressed state with deterministic tool-mediated logic**, not from prompt cleverness or context width; LLM agents are deliberately reduced to replaceable, swappable executors of decisions the engine computes.

## Purpose & intended user

The intended user is a **bug-bounty / security researcher** (in this repo, the operator running authorized HackerOne/Intigriti/direct-disclosure and OSS-CVE work) who wants to point an agent swarm at a target and get back a defensible, triage-ready report with reproducible evidence — without the agents inventing theoretical bugs, leaking the operator's PII, or going out of scope. It supports three engagement modes through one pipeline: **web** (`target_domain` → recon → live HTTP/browser hunting), **smart-contract** (6 chain families: EVM, Solana/SVM, Aptos, Sui, Substrate/ink!, CosmWasm, with live-RPC reads + fork-tested PoCs), and **OSS source audit** ("Plane O": local repo → Docker-sandboxed build/fuzz/repro). It is explicitly *human-in-the-loop*: every wave is a two-turn handshake (launch, then settle on operator re-entry), and lifecycle advances and force-merges require operator action or attested overrides.

## Architecture overview (the spine of the system)

The spine is a **single dispatch funnel over a frozen tool registry, on top of event-sourced session state**:

- **Registry & dispatch** (`mcp/lib/tool-registry.js`, `dispatch.js`): every tool is a frozen module declaring `name/inputSchema/handler/role_bundles` + capability flags (`mutating`, `network_access`, `browser_access`, `scope_required`, `sensitive_output`, `session_artifacts_written`). `executeTool` is the one path: validate args → `enforceToolPolicy` (role + 11-class authority + scope re-check) → `runWithReplaySafety` → handler → typed envelope (closed `ERROR_CODES`, versioned) → telemetry. Aliases (`bounty_*`→`bob_*`, 8-phase→6-state) are first-class with arg-adapters.
- **Lifecycle FSM** (`lifecycle-gates.js`): `SETUP → OPEN_FRONTIER ⇄ CLAIM_FREEZE → VERIFY → GRADE → REPORT`, structurally gated; advances refuse unless prerequisites pass or an operator force-overrides with a filesystem-nonce-attested token.
- **Event-sourced coordination core**: an append-only `frontier-events.jsonl` (10 event kinds) folded by deterministic, hash-stable materializers into `surface-index.json` + `task-queue.json` + a second `task-graph.json` (Plane X: typed nodes — surface/transition/hypothesis/claim — with a frozen 9-state lifecycle). Mutable state arrays were deliberately deleted; the ledger is the sole surface-state authority. Materialization is debounced once per outermost session-lock release and is best-effort (never regresses a committed write).
- **Two schedulers, one rule**: a **wave scheduler** (serialized waves of 4–8 parallel evaluator subagents) handles surface/claim work; a **graph scheduler** handles transition/hypothesis nodes via a hash-bound 3-call dispatch protocol (`prepare-node` → agent → `finalize-node`). Node kind alone routes — no second orchestrator.
- **Freeze → verify → grade → chain backbone**: a content-hashed immutable claim freeze, 3-round adversarial verification (`brutalist → balanced → final`) with a **code-computed** adjudication between rounds, a 5-axis grade producing SUBMIT/HOLD/SKIP, and chain-building (flat attempt ledger + content-addressed MCTS-style state tree).
- **Storage/concurrency** (`storage.js`): atomic temp+rename writes, reentrant stale-reclaiming session locks (synchronous-only), JSONL record caps.
- **Portability layer** (`adapters/`, `scripts/generate-*.js`): one neutral source (role prompts + registries) fans out to per-host agent/skill/settings files with `--check` CI drift gates.

## Agentic design (how/if it uses LLMs/agents: model, loop, planning, memory, tool use, single vs multi-agent)

**Multi-agent, with a deliberately dumb coordinator.** ~17 agent roles: one orchestrator, surface-discovery (normal + deep), a web evaluator, six per-chain smart-contract evaluators, OSS evaluators, three verifiers, evidence, grader, report-writer, chain-builder.

- **Loop**: the orchestrator calls `bob_start_next_wave` (the engine plans the wave via a pure bucket/cap planner), spawns one *background* subagent per assignment using the registry-named `evaluator_agent`, then **stops** (launch-turn barrier). On operator re-entry or a background-completion notification it must read state and apply the merge first (O-P8 reconciliation). Settlement never happens in the launch turn. There is no autonomous "drain the whole frontier" loop — throughput is bounded by this two-turn handshake.
- **Planning** is server-side and deterministic: priority buckets (critical/high overflow-capable, medium/low capped), two promotion mechanisms (deep surface-lead promotion; terminal-blocker loop detection with registry-delta amnesty), requeue computation. The LLM does not plan waves.
- **Memory** is the event ledger + projections + frozen artifacts; agents are stateless and crash-recoverable (any context loss is rebuilt by `resume`). Context is bounded *by construction*: summary-at-append + body-on-pull (`bob_resolve_body`), char caps on every brief slice, ≤1-hop graph context.
- **Tool use** is gated by role bundles (separation of duties — the orchestrator structurally cannot write handoffs or finalize nodes) and per-node tool-constraint detection.
- **Model routing** (from operator memory + verifier configs): spawn Opus first each wave; balanced-verifier runs on Opus, brutalist and final on Sonnet; AUP-tripped agents are silently re-spawned on Sonnet. An optional external `@brutalist/mcp` "roast" server adds a third-party adversary with graceful fallback.

## Capability inventory (bulleted, concrete)

- **Recon → routing**: 7-step shell pipeline (subfinder/httpx/katana/Wayback/nuclei/JS-extraction), deep mode adds crt.sh/dnsx/tlsx/subdomain-takeover probing; surfaces routed by frozen capability-pack registry to evaluator + brief + budget.
- **Scope-enforced HTTP** (`bob_http_scan`): SSRF-grade IPv4+IPv6 blocklist (NAT64/6to4/IPv4-mapped/SRv6), PSL registrable-domain checks, DNS-pin + per-redirect-hop re-validation, passive tech/secret/JWT/CORS/cookie/schema extraction on every response.
- **Stealth browser** (Patchright + system Chrome, human-typing, subresource pass-through, record-and-replay of page requests for OAuth/SPA capture); `evaluate` sandbox blocks covert HTTP transport.
- **Auth-differential BAC/IDOR** (structural response-signature diffing) and **doc/contract-delta** analysis (OpenAPI-3 with `$ref` resolution, Postman v2, hand-rolled GraphQL SDL parser deriving auth directives).
- **Smart contracts**: live typed reads across 6 families over curated public RPC (DNS-preflighted, credential-redacted, HTTPS-only); fork-pinned PoC runners (Foundry, Halmos symbolic, Anchor, Move ×2, cargo ×2) with hardened sandboxing (flag allowlists, env scrubbing, `$HOME`-jailed realpath, process-group kills); 7-template EVM invariant corpus.
- **OSS**: deterministic repo session identity, `.gitignore`-aware inventory as frontier events, Dockerfile generation + sandboxed runner (`--network none`, `--cap-drop ALL`, read-only root, non-root, no-secrets-in-image), read-only evidence probing with redaction; native-code HIGH gated on a live container repro (O-P4).
- **Knowledge & friction**: scored technique registry + bypass tables, 17 CLI-tool packs as pure predicates, and a closed-loop capability-friction system that mechanically widens a surface's toolset when an agent records a witnessed tool gap.
- **Verification/grading/chains**: freeze, 3-round adversarial verify with code-computed adjudication + replay leasing, 5-axis grade, content-addressed backtracking chain tree, attempt archival + cross-attempt diffing.
- **Ingestion**: audit-report parser, CVE feed parser + scope matcher (paste-only, no fetch), HackerOne public-intel CVE-to-surface matching.
- **Safety/ops**: temp-email tool (operator-PII guardrail), audit-graded path protection, telemetry, doctor/uninstall, 4-runtime portability.

## Knowledge system (how security/vuln knowledge is encoded & retrieved)

Knowledge is **data, not prose-in-prompts**, deliberately so it is testable and budget-bounded. Two parallel stores feed one deterministic scorer:

- **Bypass tables** (`.hacker-bob/bypass-tables/*.txt`): terse one-line payload primers for ~8 bug classes (jwt, oauth-oidc, graphql, ssrf, nextjs, rest-api, wordpress, firebase) — well-known technique vocabulary, read directly.
- **Technique registry** (`evaluator-techniques.json`, ~11 entries) + **JS technique packs** (`technique-packs.js`, incl. a cross-stack web2↔web3 identity-handoff pack with worked witness templates). `scoreTechniqueEntry` does weighted **substring matching** (tech ×8, endpoints ×5, hints ×4, params ×3), selects under a 6000-char budget, falls back to `generic-rest-api`. Full-pack reads are metered against a per-session budget (`technique-pack-reads.jsonl`).

Retrieval is surface-keyed: the same metadata that routes the evaluator selects its knowledge slice, which is injected into the bounded assignment brief. A **friction feedback loop** is the one self-improving mechanism: an agent (or an adversarial transcript scanner) records "I needed tool X," which — past a threshold, with a mechanical witness and idempotency — promotes a pack-widening so the next run of that surface lands with the tool present. **Honest limit**: this improves *tool availability*, not *technique quality*; the corpus is small (~17 KB, hand-curated, last-edited timestamps), scoring is naive substring (no embeddings/CVE feed/exploit-DB), and the wave/surface scored-JSON model and the graph/node JS-pack model are two partially-overlapping code paths.

## Verification, validation & safety

This is the strongest-engineered part of the system.

- **Freeze-then-verify**: verification runs only on an immutable, content-hashed claim batch; post-freeze mutations to findings/chains/auth/surface cannot change a verdict.
- **Adversarial by construction**: brutalist and balanced rounds are independent (never see each other), must cover the snapshot finding-ids exactly, and are merged by a **deterministic code adjudicator** that forces re-replay on disagreement, high/critical agreement, state-sensitivity, low confidence, small batches, and a hash-seeded QA sample. The LLM judges; the engine computes diffs, hashes, and gates. Full hash lineage (`freeze → snapshot → rounds → adjudication → final → evidence`) is reviewer-recomputable; evidence completeness is re-derived from on-disk artifacts so tampering surfaces as missing/mismatched.
- **Replay hygiene**: cross-process file leases (TTL + heartbeat + PID liveness) serialize unsafe replays per capability pack.
- **Grade**: arithmetic + threshold consistency is server-validated (SUBMIT ≥40 + a reportable medium+, HOLD 20–39, SKIP otherwise).
- **Safety guardrails**: SSRF blocklists + DNS-pinning; scope re-checked on every session-bound call and every redirect; audit-graded paths are MCP-rendered (agents never Write reports/chains/grades); PII/secret screening in contracts and a hard project rule against ever submitting operator personal data (a temp-email tool exists for this). Smart-contract surfaces cannot be marked "complete" without a finding or a cited bypass attempt (anti-low-effort-attestation).

**Honest gaps**: confirmed chains **cannot elevate a finding's severity** (no `elevated_severity` field exists — the ladder lives only in the chain-builder prompt; a chained CRITICAL adds at most 15 grade points). Grade *quality* is fully LLM-trusted (only the math is checked). The external "roast" adversary can silently drop out. The final round only re-runs reportables. Replay leases are host-local. Auto-freeze fallback can mask missing operator intent.

## Standout strengths (ranked)

1. **MCP-owned, content-addressed event sourcing with a thin replaceable LLM** — the central design bet, executed thoroughly: deterministic hash-stable views, idempotent stigmergic coordination of many short-lived agents with no message bus, full replay/audit/crash-recovery.
2. **Genuinely adversarial verification** — independent rounds + code-computed adjudication + replay leasing is hard to fake and rare in agent frameworks; verdict math is never LLM-trusted.
3. **Defense-in-depth safety** — SSRF/scope hardening, sandboxed subprocess execution (smart-contract + OSS runners), audit-graded immutability, PII guardrails, fail-closed everywhere.
4. **Multi-chain breadth with per-family correctness** — 6 families modeled at the protocol/address/fork/provenance/bug-taxonomy level, not a thin EVM wrapper; three EVM PoC engines (concrete + symbolic + invariant).
5. **Registry-driven single-source-of-truth + `--check` drift gates** — one change fans out coherently to tools, permissions, agent frontmatter, and 4 host adapters; CI fails on hand-edits.
6. **Context discipline by construction** — summary-at-append/body-on-pull, char/token budgets, ≤1-hop graph context — solves a real agent-framework failure mode.

## Real limitations (ranked, honest)

1. **Severity ceiling — the dominant, operator-acknowledged limit.** No engine path elevates severity via chains, and the OSS reachability classifier (`attack_vector`/`severity_ceiling` producers) that would lift findings above MEDIUM is **on an unmerged branch, not on `main`** — the brief consumers exist but nothing stamps the fields. Result: the system reliably lands MED/LOW, not HIGH/CRITICAL, on its own.
2. **Throughput is human-gated and strictly serialized** — one wave in flight, 4–8 surfaces/wave, two-turn handshake per wave, no pipelining; one dead background agent stalls the pipeline until operator force-merge. No autonomous drain loop.
3. **Knowledge is thin and static** — small hand-curated corpus, naive substring scoring, no CVE/exploit-DB ingestion, no learning of *new* techniques; the friction loop only fixes tool gaps.
4. **Heavy mid-migration cruft** — pervasive v1/v2 + "LEGACY: removed in Plane D" dual-write windows, legacy field backfills, naming collisions (two "promotions", `bob_chain_frontier` vs event-frontier, generic Contract-DSL vs on-chain contracts), and lazy `require` cycles — real cognitive overhead and drift surface.
5. **Capability gaps in the audit engines** — invariant generation and symbolic execution are EVM-only; no general-purpose SAST (`bob_static_scan` is EVM/Solana token-rug only); non-EVM "live mainnet" forking is materially weaker; OSS fuzzing has known aarch64 libFuzzer/compiler-rt friction and fixed 4 GB run memory.
6. **Adapter inconsistency** — the Kimi adapter is partially stale/parked (references retired role IDs, throws on generation, still uses legacy `bountyagent` server key + `bounty_*` tools); Codex/Kimi lack the Claude PreToolUse scope guards (safety leans on MCP-side validation there); generic-MCP is doc-only with no drift gate.
7. **O(events) folds and a blunt 18k-event ledger ceiling** with "rotate the session" as the only remedy, and best-effort materialization that can silently leave views stale.

## Maturity (tests/docs/activity/production-readiness)

**Notably mature for a security-research tool.** ~170 `mcp/lib` modules, ~148 registered tools, ~141 test files, and a comprehensive `npm test` gate covering MCP unit tests, prompt/skill/settings/stigmergy **`--check` drift coherence**, install-smoke + legacy-upgrade, CLI/adapter-detection/package, **policy-replay** (authority decisions), and hook write/read-guard tests in Python. Minimal, current dependencies (`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`, `proxy-agent`, `psl`, optional `patchright`), Node ≥20, Apache-2.0. Documentation is substantial and matches the code: 14 `docs/*.md` including `SMART_CONTRACTS_SPEC.md`, `OSS_MODE_MVP.md`, `ADAPTERS.md`, `context-scaling-architecture.md`, `capability-hypergraph.md`, plane designs, a roadmap, and a benchmark plan; project-level `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` enforce registry-driven, MCP-owned-state invariants.

Activity is high and ongoing (recent Dependabot merges; operator memory shows continuous tuning, an active `surface-discovery`/v2.1 line, and real disclosure output — multiple CVEs and GHSAs filed via this pipeline). **Production-readiness caveat**: it is genuinely used for authorized engagements and produces filed, accepted findings, but it remains a **single-operator, human-gated research framework**, not a turnkey autonomous scanner — and it is mid-migration (v1/v2, unmerged severity-ceiling work, a parked Kimi adapter), so the live `main` capability is meaningfully below the documented architectural ambition.

---

### Profile: raptor

## Identity (one paragraph: what this project IS, plainly)

RAPTOR ("Recursive Autonomous Penetration Testing and Observation Robot") is a large, autonomous **source- and binary-code security-research framework built on top of Claude Code**, authored by a notable team (Gadi Evron, Daniel Cuthbert, Thomas Dullien/Halvar Flake, Michael Bargury, John Cartwright). It chains static analysis (Semgrep + CodeQL + Coccinelle), software-composition analysis (SCA), binary fuzzing (AFL++/libFuzzer), LLM-driven vulnerability validation, SMT/Z3 feasibility reasoning, exploit-PoC generation, and patch writing into one pipeline you point at a codebase, archive, or binary. It is genuinely mature for the SAST/OSS/native domain (~301K non-test Python LOC + ~285K test LOC across ~905–916 `test_*.py` files, ~16,700 test functions, 16 CI workflows), and decidedly **not** a live-web/bounty tool: the README is self-aware ("not polished software... held together with enthusiasm and duct tape") and the `/web` module is an explicit alpha stub.

## Purpose & intended user

Built for offensive/defensive security researchers and OSS maintainers working on **C/C++/native code, memory-corruption exploitability, supply-chain risk, and OSS forensics**. It serves two distinct user modes from one codebase: (1) a **headless CI/automation user** who runs `python3 raptor.py scan --repo ...` and gets SARIF/SBOM/VEX with no Claude Code at all, and (2) an **interactive researcher** who drives the full agentic loop (scan→validate→exploit→patch) via slash commands inside Claude Code. Its center of gravity is finding *real, exploitable, reachable* bugs in source/binaries and proving them — not crawling live web targets.

## Architecture overview (the spine of the system)

The defining design decision is a **two-layer split**, stated bluntly in `CLAUDE.md`: *"Python orchestrates everything. Claude shows results concisely. Never circumvent Python execution flow."*

- **Python execution layer** (`raptor.py` 54KB thin launcher/dispatcher; `raptor_agentic.py` 134KB real orchestrator; `raptor_codeql.py`; `raptor_fuzzing.py`; plus `packages/`, `core/`, `engine/`): "It does not make decisions. It executes." Runs tools, parses SARIF, dedups, dispatches LLM calls, tracks cost, writes JSON/JSONL artifacts.
- **Claude Code decision layer** (`CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/` tree, `tiers/`): "makes the calls" — prioritization, realism judgments, exploit reasoning.

There is **no single while-loop agent**; control is **phase-staged subprocess orchestration**. The headline `/agentic` pipeline runs: Phase 0 mitigation analysis → Phase 1 Semgrep+CodeQL (parallel) + `/understand` and reachability pre-passes → Phase 1b SCA → Phase 2 exploitability validation + dedup → Phase 3 autonomous LLM prep → Phase 4 multi-model orchestration (analysis→consensus→judge→aggregate) → Phase 4-post `/validate` → Phase 5 fuzzing confirmation. Each phase is subprocess-isolated (with a `--phase-timeout` knob) and writes JSON artifacts consumed by the next.

RAPTOR is itself a **full Claude-Code framework**: it ships `.claude/settings.json`, ~27 slash commands, ~16 sub-agents, and skills. Crucially — and unlike Hacker Bob — its "tools" are **plain allowlisted shell scripts under `libexec/`, not an MCP server**. Slash-command dispatch is metadata-driven (`dispatch:` frontmatter, CI-enforced by `check_command_metadata.py`); lifecycle hooks (`SessionStart`/`Stop`/`SessionEnd`/`PostToolUseFailure`) wire Claude Code events to run lifecycle management. Durable state is **JSON/JSONL files governed by convention** (schema validators, CI metadata checks) — there is **no owned state-authority server**. Two oracle-polymorphic records unify verification: `Witness` (SHA-256-keyed triggering bytes + outcome) and `VerifiedOutcome` ("Finding + verifying-oracle + status + oracle-tagged evidence," deliberately not shaped around any one oracle).

## Agentic design (how/if it uses LLMs/agents)

RAPTOR layers **three distinct "agent" paradigms** with no unified abstraction (a real architectural smell):

1. **A mostly-deterministic macro pipeline** (`raptor_agentic.py:main()`, a ~2000-line monolith). Targets come from *scanners* (Semgrep/CodeQL findings), ranked by dataflow-bearing-first + operator `--prefer` globs + target-type catalogs — **not** chosen by a model. This is the key point: despite "Truly Agentic" branding, **target selection is scanner-driven, not autonomously strategic.** There is no agent forming hypotheses about *where* to look.
2. **A clean, provider-agnostic tool-use agent loop** (`core/llm/tool_use/loop.py`, ~972 LOC) — the best-engineered piece in the codebase. A single budgeted `run_with_history()` (cost/wall-clock/token/context-window gates), pairing-aware truncation, terminal-tool pattern for structured escape, used only for **bounded** sub-tasks: variant hunting, dataflow tracing, CVE-fix-commit discovery (search space is always one given pattern/finding/CVE).
3. **A per-finding structured LLM pipeline** (`AutonomousSecurityAgentV2`) that does analysis/exploit/patch via schema-constrained `generate_structured` calls — explicitly *bypassing* the nice ToolUseLoop and re-implementing retry/validation.

**Multi-provider & multi-model:** Anthropic (default `claude-opus-4-6`, fast tier `claude-haiku-4-5`, newer models like `claude-opus-4-8` registered), OpenAI (gpt-5.x/4.x/o1/o3), Gemini 2.5, Ollama, plus two Claude-Code-as-provider shims. **Role-based** model assignment (`analysis`/`consensus`/`judge`/`aggregate`/`fallback`) with task-type routing (binary verdicts → cheap tier). Phase 4 is genuine multi-agent: parallel per-finding analysis → blind consensus vote → non-blind judge (sees pre-consensus primary verdict) → aggregate narrative, with anti-collusion cross-family checks and a multi-model-collapse guard. **Memory** is thin: no persistent cross-turn agent memory beyond the message list, but two cross-run stores exist — SAGE ("consensus-validated persistence" across projects/runs) and Tier-3 verified-outcome retrieval (primes prompts with RAPTOR's own prior confirmed exploits as exemplars). Fuzzing's "autonomous" planner is largely **vestigial** — it ranks crashes but never closes the loop on the live fuzzer (the campaign is a fixed-duration `sleep(10)` poll).

## Capability inventory (bulleted, concrete)

- **Three-engine static pipeline** layered by cost/depth: Semgrep (broad/cheap) → CodeQL (deep interprocedural; primary detector + IRIS query packs) → Coccinelle/`spatch` (C/kernel structural "source intelligence": allocation shapes, privilege boundaries, abort-dominance, 15 GCC-attribute extractors).
- **IRIS LocalFlowSource CodeQL packs** (Python/Java/JS/Go; CWE-22/78/79/89/94/502/611/918) using a *broader* source model so **absence of flow = refutation** (free downgrade before any LLM spend), guarded by a `CallableInventory.ql` probe distinguishing genuine 0-match from extractor drop.
- **Z3/SMT two-layer integration:** CodeQL path-constraint satisfiability (drop `unsat` paths pre-LLM, feed `sat` concrete inputs into prompts) + one-gadget register/memory feasibility ranking against crash state. C-semantics modeled (`umul_overflows` for CWE-190, width coercion, shift disambiguation).
- **Binary-oracle reachability suppression** (DWARF + nm): classifies functions `symbol_present`/`inlined`/`folded`/`absent`; `absent ∧ ¬inlined ⇒ dead code` hard-suppressed pre-LLM (never overriding a sound witness), with provenance gate, source-coverage floor, and `suppressions.jsonl` audit trail.
- **Host-native fuzzing** (AFL++ + libFuzzer): capability-aware fuzzer auto-selection (real macOS shmem probe, Apple-clang-no-libFuzzer fallback), CmpLog/QEMU/dictionary/custom-mutator flags, live JSONL telemetry + plateau detection, radare2 reachability-weighted prioritization (transitive dangerous-sink distance), crash → canonical `Witness` with build-hash binding.
- **SCA / supply-chain arc:** manifests + lockfiles + Dockerfile base images → OSV/KEV/EPSS/SSVC enrichment → CycloneDX SBOM + SARIF + VEX, with a **self-calibrating risk model** (CI grid-searches multipliers against a corpus).
- **OSS forensics** pipeline (GH Archive/BigQuery, Wayback, GitHub API, local git; 9 dedicated investigator agents).
- **Cost discipline:** free deterministic refutation gates (IRIS Tier-1, SMT unsat, fixture-detection, reachability chokepoints) that skip paid LLM calls; **Wilson-95%-bound fast-tier scorecard** (cheap sibling model prefilters confident FPs only, trust per `(model, decision_class)` cell, activates at ≤5% upper-bound miss-rate with shadow sampling).
- **Exploit/PoC machinery:** compile-verify (`compiled ∈ {True,False,None}`), intent-match judge (heuristic-first, LLM tiebreak), "inline the trigger" PoC contract, default-OFF `--execute-exploits`.
- **Offline-first:** Semgrep registry packs vendored under `engine/semgrep/rules/registry-cache/` — no network at scan time.
- **`/web`: alpha stub** — SSRF rule dir empty, offensive kit (`SecOpsAgentKit`) an uninitialized submodule, no real dynamic web engine.

## Knowledge system (how security/vuln knowledge is encoded & retrieved)

Knowledge is **multi-modal and heavily declarative**, across distinct strata:

- **Engine corpora (mechanical):** ~21 first-party Semgrep YAMLs by class (crypto deepest at ~12 rules) + 7 cached registry packs; CodeQL `.ql`/`.qll` with `@tags external/cwe/cwe-NNN`, indexed by `(language, CWE)`; dozens of Coccinelle `.cocci` rules encoding C/kernel idioms (allocator names, abort macros, capability constants embedded directly in SmPL).
- **CWE-strategy YAML playbooks** (`core/llm/cwe_strategies/strategies/*.yml`, 8 bug-class files): each a real playbook with `signals` (paths/includes/keywords/CWE list), `key_questions` (decision lenses), `prompt_addendum`, and CVE `exemplars`. A transparent **picker** scores strategies, pinning +100 on CWE-id match, and injects top-3 rendered strategies *outside the data zone* (after the closing `</pattern>` tag) so they're trusted guidance, not data.
- **`tiers/` progressive-disclosure system:** token-budgeted prose doctrine. Guidance files auto-load by trigger keyword (`analysis-guidance`, `exploit-guidance` with bad-byte/calling-convention/bypass tables, `recovery`/`validation-recovery` failure→action maps); 9 named-expert **personas** (Mark Dowd, Halvar Flake, Charlie Miller, etc.) load on-demand at "0 tokens until invoked" and are *extracted from the Python `agent.py` prompts* to stay in sync. `specialists/`/`reference/` are empty placeholders.
- **CVE/supply-chain corpora:** `core/cve/` (EPSS/KEV/SSVC), a `cvefix` pipeline learning sanitizer/barrier patterns from real fixes, and executable ground-truth incident corpora (Log4Shell, ua-parser-js, event-stream...) with machine-checkable `expected.yaml` contracts.
- **Self-generated knowledge:** confirmed findings feed back as Tier-3 verified-outcome exemplars; KNighter-style checker synthesis turns one confirmed bug into a variant-hunting rule.

## Verification, validation & safety

Verification is **RAPTOR's center of gravity and strongest theme**. The `/validate` exploitability pipeline is an explicit state machine (Stages 0→A→B→C→D→E→F) with a sharp convention — **letters = pure LLM reasoning, numbers = mechanical Python**, each stage schema-validated. Named **MUST-GATEs** target real LLM failure modes: GATE-4 [NO-HEDGING] ("if/maybe/could potentially" must be immediately verified), GATE-5 [FULL-COVERAGE] ("do not sample or guess"), GATE-8 [POC-EVIDENCE] ("'ran without error' is not evidence"), plus a PROXIMITY 0-10 value-level prediction gate ("RIP=0x4141..., SIGSEGV" not "supplying 24+ bytes overwrites the return address").

The validation funnel runs **cheapest-first**: structural function-exists pre-check → free IRIS/SMT/fixture/reachability refutation gates → LLM dataflow validation → compile-verify → intent-match judge → multi-model consensus/judge → semantic-entropy confidence (Jaccard divergence over reasoning = low-confidence flag) → optional fuzzing.

**Agent-self-security is best-in-class for this space:** Landlock+seccomp+mount-ns sandbox (Seatbelt on macOS) with fail-loud `SANDBOX_ENGAGE_EXIT_CODE`; per-call nonce prompt-injection envelope (academically grounded, datamarking, untrusted-bytes-in-user-role); two-layer tool-result injection defense with named-corpus refuse-list validated at construction; **x-source provenance enforcement** (tool-input fields tagged `discovered` are blocked unless the value appeared in the prompt or prior successful tool output — a genuine loop-level anti-hallucination guard); credential isolation via socket+inheritable-token-FD; Rule-of-Two CI gate (process-tree HITL detection, not `isatty`); trust-gating of analyzer configs (`codeql_trust.py` refuses repo-supplied `buildCommand`/symlinks; `--trust-repo` only, never env-driven); `poc_source_scan` rejecting `#include "/etc/passwd"`/traversal/`__has_include` oracles before gcc; trust-marker self-guard on every `libexec/` script; `.semgrepignore` for intentionally-vulnerable fixtures.

**Honest verification gaps:** with **defaults**, a fuzzing/exploit finding can be marked `exploitable` on the LLM's boolean + a PoC that merely *compiles* — actual reproduction requires `--execute-exploits` (off by default). So default verification is **weaker than the prompts imply: compile-success ≠ reproduction.** The much-touted multi-axis source-intel grading is **largely aspirational** — `adapter.py` returns `UNCERTAIN` for most inputs ("Phase 2 ships exactly one axis"); the docstrings describe far more capability than is wired into verdicts. Adopt the architecture, not the precision claims.

## Standout strengths (ranked)

1. **The clean execution/decision split** — headless-CI-runnable Python core + separable LLM brain, with a *real* dual-mode payoff (SARIF in CI *or* full agentic interactively). Architecturally disciplined and rare.
2. **Statistically-honest self-calibration** — binary-oracle suppression ships held-out generalization numbers with rule-of-three upper bounds (1952/1952 + 187/187 held-out, miss-rate UB ≤1.6%); scorecard short-circuits only under a Wilson 95% bound. Research-grade rigor about its *own* reliability.
3. **Free deterministic refutation before paid LLM work** (IRIS Tier-1, SMT unsat, fixture/reachability gates) — the right economic instinct, instrumented with savings counters.
4. **SMT-before-LLM path pruning + concrete-witness extraction** — makes "unreachable" provable rather than a model's guess, and feeds real trigger inputs into prompts.
5. **Defense-in-depth against hostile target code/tool output** — sandbox, nonce prompt envelope, x-source provenance, Rule-of-Two, trust-gating, PoC source-scan, credential isolation. Treats target and tool output as adversarial throughout.
6. **The `ToolUseLoop`** — exhaustively budgeted, provider-agnostic, pairing-aware truncation; reusable infrastructure, not a one-off.
7. **Layered verification depth** (compile-verify + intent-match + consensus + semantic-entropy + fuzzing) — far more rigorous than a single LLM verdict.
8. **Regression-driven engineering culture** — docstrings narrate the exact bug each guard prevents; CI regression-tests findings against a real-incident corpus.

## Real limitations (ranked, honest)

1. **Not autonomously strategic.** Despite the branding, the macro flow is scanner-driven; "agency" is confined to bounded enumeration sub-tasks. No agent hypothesizes *where* to look or pivots on what it learns — this is where Hacker Bob's wave/frontier model is genuinely more exploratory.
2. **Web/bounty is effectively absent.** `/web` is an alpha stub, SSRF rules empty, offensive kit an uninitialized submodule. For live-web work RAPTOR is non-functional out of the box.
3. **Three overlapping "agent" paradigms** with no unified abstraction, and `raptor_agentic.py:main()` is a **~2000-line procedural monolith** — the highest-risk maintenance surface.
4. **Heavily C/C++/native-centric** — exploits as `.cpp`, compile-verify gated on C/C++, source-intel/personas/SMT verbs all biased to memory corruption. Web/auth/business-logic depth is thin.
5. **Default verification proves compilation, not reproduction** (fuzzing/exploit path) — and the rich multi-axis source-intel grading is mostly unshipped (`UNCERTAIN` for most inputs).
6. **No MCP / no owned state authority** — durable state is JSON/JSONL governed by convention, with many best-effort `except Exception: pass` blocks trading observability for robustness.
7. **Heavy host-dependency surface** (~6GB devcontainer: Semgrep, CodeQL [non-commercial license], AFL++, r2, GDB, Z3, BigQuery creds). Most power features degrade *silently* when a tool is missing.
8. **No capability benchmark anywhere.** It measures *regression* exhaustively but publishes **no** precision/recall/F1 or comparative eval — "does it catch known-bad" is gated; "what's its false-positive rate" is unmeasured.
9. **Phased/incomplete subsystems in-tree** (credential isolation mid-rollout, scorecard wired only into CodeQL, fuzzing autonomy loop doesn't close, harness generation refuses to auto-run, many fuzz target-kinds detection-only).

## Maturity (tests/docs/activity/production-readiness)

**Very high for the SAST/CodeQL/SCA/fuzzing engine; uneven at the agent-facing edges.** ~905–916 `test_*.py` files, ~16,700 test functions, ~285K test LOC (roughly 1:1 with source), across ~90 colocated `tests/` dirs. Test distribution is heavily weighted to deterministic detectors (SCA ~240 files, source_intel/codeql/dataflow hundreds of `test_` each) and **thinner on the LLM-driven agentic/exploit core** relative to its size. The CI/test apparatus is itself a first-class engineered subsystem: 16 workflows (~3,554 LOC) with tiered `pytest-split`, container-vs-runner fork fallback, SHA-pinned actions, `persist-credentials: false`, a single stable `tests-passed` aggregate gate, and **self-validating CI** (import-graph path-filter test with autofixer, a shell harness testing the release YAML, trust-marker coverage tests). Standout maturity signals: finding-regression against a real-incident corpus with `must_detect` contracts, silent-regression stress-sweep diffing against a committed baseline, self-dogfooding SCA-self-bump, and ground-truth calibration crons (KEV/EPSS refresh, monthly risk-model refit). Docs are substantial and current (`ARCHITECTURE.md` 41KB, `DATAFLOW_VALIDATION_SUMMARY.md`, `FUZZING_QUICKSTART.md`, `CITATION.cff` v3.0.0 with ORCIDs). The product README's "duct tape" framing contradicts the actual engineering depth — the **engine is production-grade and disciplined; the agent-facing breadth (web, offsec kit, `tiers/` placeholders, several mid-rollout subsystems) lags**, and there is no quantitative effectiveness benchmark to cite.

---

### Profile: CyberStrikeAI

## Identity (one paragraph: what this project IS, plainly)

CyberStrikeAI (`Ed1s0nZ/CyberStrikeAI`, Go module `cyberstrike-ai`, Go 1.24) is a self-hosted, web-first **AI-native offensive-security platform**: a single Go (gin) binary that wraps ~89 declarative CLI-tool recipes (nmap, sqlmap, nuclei, metasploit, ghidra, hydra, impacket, bloodhound…), a built-in Go command-and-control (C2) framework, an AntSword-class WebShell manager, a host PTY/reverse-shell capability, a RAG knowledge base, and an Anthropic-Agent-Skills layer behind an LLM agent loop, driven conversationally through a vanilla-JS SPA (or Burp/DingTalk/Lark/WeChat bridges). It is **not** a CLI batch scanner and **not** an autonomous hunt-to-report pipeline — it is an operator-driven, server-resident red-team console where a human types natural-language pentest instructions and watches a streamed agentic run. It is a Chinese-first community project (404Starlink member, Tencent hackathon badge) with bilingual docs.

## Purpose & intended user

The intended user is a **security operator / red-teamer** who wants an LLM to plan and execute offensive engagements end-to-end against targets they control or are authorized to test. The product loop is "describe the target in chat → the agent selects tool recipes, shells out to locally-installed binaries, streams results, narrates an attack-chain graph, and records vulnerabilities." It explicitly spans the full kill chain including post-exploitation (C2, persistence, lateral movement, exfiltration, opsec-evasion, cleanup), so it targets full-engagement red-team / pentest work, **not** scoped bug-bounty disclosure. Safety rests on operator trust plus access control (auth + optional HITL), not on scope enforcement or finding verification.

## Architecture overview (the spine of the system)

One Go monolith. A gin router (`internal/app/app.go`, ~74KB) sits behind Bearer-token auth, persists everything to SQLite (`data/conversations.db`, `data/knowledge.db`), and serves a zero-build vanilla-JS SPA (~73K LOC JS/CSS; `chat.js` 8.2K LOC, `monitor.js` 5.3K LOC). The agent engine is **ByteDance CloudWeGo Eino ADK** (`github.com/cloudwego/eino v0.8.13` + `eino-ext`) — native ReAct was removed 2026-06-02, so everything now runs through Eino's prebuilt `deep`/`supervisor`/`planexecute` orchestrators. Request flow: SPA/Burp/robot → HTTP/SSE handler → `multiagent.RunDeepAgent` builds an `einoopenai.ChatModel` + sub-agents as `adk.NewChatModelAgent`, wraps them in the chosen orchestrator, then `runEinoADKAgentLoop` pulls `iter.Next()` over an `AsyncIterator[*adk.AgentEvent]` and **translates Eino events into SSE frames**. Tools are bridged uniformly via `internal/einomcp` (`mcpBridgeTool`) regardless of whether they are YAML CLI wrappers, native Go tools, or federated remote MCP servers, and execute through `internal/security/executor.go`, which shells out (`exec.CommandContext`). Key `internal/` packages: `multiagent/` (the engine, 43 files), `agent/` (single-agent + tool registry), `agents/` (markdown loader), `mcp/`+`einomcp/` (server/host/bridge), `knowledge/` (RAG), `c2/` (18 files), `security/` (executor/ratelimit/auth), `handler/` (~28K LOC, largest), plus `attackchain/`, `reasoning/`, `vision/`, `robot/`, `skillpackage/`, `project/`. Concurrency is one `adk.Runner` per HTTP request with a synchronous outer event loop; cross-agent parallelism lives inside Eino, not in a custom scheduler.

## Agentic design (how/if it uses LLMs/agents)

- **Model:** OpenAI-compatible by default, shipped pointing at Alibaba DashScope `qwen3-max` (`config.yaml:56,59`); `provider: claude` transparently bridges to the Anthropic Claude Messages API (including extended-thinking mapping) via a custom transport. `internal/reasoning/eino.go` maps reasoning-effort intent to provider-specific fields (Claude adaptive/budget thinking, DeepSeek `thinking`, OpenAI `reasoning_effort`). **One `baseModelCfg` is reused for every agent — there is no Opus-orchestrator/Sonnet-worker tiering.**
- **Loop:** Eino ADK's, not hand-rolled. CyberStrikeAI's run loop is an event-translator/UX layer handling streaming-delta normalization, tool-call fragment merging/dedup, pending-tool-call recovery, panic recovery, checkpoint resume, and transient-retry segmentation. `agent.max_iterations: 12000` (effectively unbounded ReAct).
- **Single vs multi-agent:** Both. Four runtime-selectable topologies over the same definitions: `eino_single` (one ChatModelAgent), `deep` (coordinator + `task` tool spawning context-isolated sub-agents — the flagship), `plan_execute` (Eino Planner→Executor→Replanner, outer loop cap default 10, with a `lenientPlan` parser that fabricates a default step on total parse failure), and `supervisor` (`transfer`/`exit` handoffs).
- **Planning:** Prompt-driven in deep/supervisor (orchestrator told to `write_todos` then fan out); structurally enforced by Eino's typed plan in plan_execute.
- **Memory:** SQLite conversations/messages + a **"project blackboard"** split into `upsert_project_fact` (reproduction context, incl. dead ends) vs `record_vulnerability` (deliverable findings), with a blackboard index injected each turn and a mandated "record-as-you-go" rhythm to survive compaction. Plus a serious context-engineering middleware stack: summarization-by-ratio (trigger 0.8), large-result reduction (truncate/spill to disk >50KB, clear old results >160K tokens), orphan-tool-message pruning (keeps token telemetry consistent with actual model input), dynamic **tool_search** (only ~12 tools resident, rest regex-unlocked on demand), and file-based checkpoint/resume.
- **Tool use:** ~130 MCP-surfaced tools total — ~89 YAML CLI wrappers + ~46 built-in Go tools (vuln recording, 6-tool fact blackboard, KB search, vision, 9-tool WebShell, 13-tool batch queue, 8-tool C2, filesystem `exec/read/write/edit`). Sub-agents see only the `task.description`, not the parent transcript; orchestrator prompts compensate with a mandatory pre-delegation completeness gate (target ID + scope + single goal + success criteria) and a "colleague who just walked in" handoff discipline that the recipient re-checks.
- **Recursive agent composition:** `mcp-servers/pent_claude_agent/` wraps the **Anthropic Claude Agent SDK** as five MCP tools, so the Go orchestrator can delegate a whole sub-engagement to a nested Claude Code agent with its own model/key/allowed-tools — MCP-over-MCP.

## Capability inventory (bulleted, concrete)

- **~89 declarative CLI-tool packs** (`tools/*.yaml`): recon (nmap, masscan, rustscan, amass, subfinder, fscan), web (sqlmap, nuclei, dalfox, ffuf, katana, wpscan, zap, jwt-analyzer, graphql-scanner), exploitation/AD (metasploit, msfvenom, hydra, hashcat, impacket, netexec, responder, smbmap), binary/pwn (ghidra, radare2, gdb, ropgadget, pwntools, angr, checksec), forensics/stego (volatility3, binwalk, exiftool, steghide, zsteg), cloud/container (prowler, scout-suite, pacu, trivy, checkov, kube-bench), OSINT (shodan, fofa, quake, zoomeye). Each is a CLI-arg-mapping DSL (`format: positional|flag|template`, defaults, `required`, `allowed_exit_codes`) auto-compiled to JSON Schema.
- **Built-in Go C2 framework** (`internal/c2/`): tcp_reverse / http_beacon / https_beacon / websocket listeners, AES-256-GCM Go-templated beacon (`beacon.go.tmpl`, CGO-free, silent-fail), SQLite session/task queues, restored-after-restart, optional HITL gating.
- **Standalone Python MCP servers** (`mcp-servers/`): `reverse_shell` (real TCP reverse-shell listener as 5 MCP tools, binds `0.0.0.0`) and `pent_claude_agent` (Claude Agent SDK as a nested pentester).
- **MCP triple-role:** ships its own JSON-RPC MCP server over stdio/HTTP/SSE (~1450 LOC, hand-rolled); is an MCP host that federates third-party servers via the official Go SDK with `name::tool` namespacing + health/call-count monitoring; publishes standalone reusable MCP servers. Soft-error tool protocol (`__CYBERSTRIKE_AI_TOOL_ERROR__` prefix + unknown-tool reminder return `nil` errors so the loop self-corrects).
- **WebShell manager** (IceSword/AntSword-compatible PHP/ASP/ASPX/JSP) + browser virtual terminal running a **real host PTY** (`creack/pty`).
- **Web SPA**: 17+ pages incl. reconnectable live SSE timeline (`TaskEventBus` mirrors frames so refreshed pages / second tabs re-attach mid-run), inline HITL approve/reject/edit-args cards, cancel-one-tool-with-a-note that re-enters model context, MCP execution monitor with trend charts, dashboard KPIs.
- **Vision** `analyze_image` (separate VL model; image bytes deliberately kept out of agent history — only text summaries injected).
- **RAG knowledge base**, **24-ish skills (22 vuln-class playbooks + 1 demo)**, **13 role personas**, **16 markdown agents**, batch/scheduled tasks (cron), DingTalk/Lark/WeChat robots, Burp Suite extension (REST/SSE client, not MCP), attack-chain SVG graph, audit log, OTel tracing, bilingual i18n (~2,840 keys/locale).

## Knowledge system (how security/vuln knowledge is encoded & retrieved)

Four-to-five **parallel, mostly-disconnected** knowledge substrates, all human-authored files, none auto-generated or verified:

1. **Tool recipes** (`tools/*.yaml`, 89) — *how to invoke*, not *when*; knowledge lives in freeform `description` (operational gotchas like "-O OS detect requires root", "full-port scan is slow"). Registered wholesale per role, not per-surface.
2. **Roles** (`roles/*.yaml`, 13) — Chinese-named personas (渗透测试/pentest, 信息收集/recon, CTF, 云安全审计/cloud…) = a `user_prompt` prefix + a `tools` allowlist. This is the only "selection" logic, and it is static per-persona.
3. **Agents** (`agents/*.md`, 16 = 3 orchestrators + 13 kill-chain sub-agents) — YAML front-matter (`name/id/tools/bind_role/max_iterations/kind`) + a markdown body that becomes the system prompt verbatim. `bind_role` lets a markdown sub-agent inherit a YAML role's tool whitelist (one permission registry across single/multi-agent). Notably shipped sub-agents often have empty `tools: []` and no `bind_role`, so they inherit the global set — the binding feature is under-used.
4. **Skills** (`skills/*/SKILL.md`, 23 dirs = 22 vuln-class playbooks + 1 `cyberstrike-eino-demo`) — faithful, *validated* Anthropic Agent-Skills with **progressive disclosure**: names/descriptions listed in context, full `SKILL.md` pulled on demand via Eino's `skill` tool; bundles can ship runnable `scripts/`. Spec-validated (`validate.go` forbids reserved "anthropic"/"claude" names), though 22/24 violate their own stated front-matter spec by including a `version:` field.
5. **RAG corpus** (`knowledge_base/`) — PayloadsAllTheThings-style markdown, but **only 2 categories** (Prompt Injection ~210 lines; SQL Injection ~3,400 lines per-DBMS) and **disabled by default** (`knowledge.enabled: false`). Engine is a clean Eino pipeline (markdown/recursive splitter → tiktoken-aware chunking → OpenAI-compatible embedder → SQLite) but retrieval is **brute-force O(N) cosine over a full table scan**, decoding every embedding from JSON per query (no ANN). Good correctness touches: embedding dimension/model-name row-skip guards, two-step `list_knowledge_risk_types`→scoped `search_knowledge_base` UX, token-budgeted dedupe. Threshold inconsistency (code default 0.7 vs config 0.4). Skills and tool descriptions are **not** in the RAG index, so the most actionable methodology is never semantically retrievable.

Net: a playbook/recipe corpus, **not** a rule engine — no CVE DB, no Semgrep/CodeQL queries, no surface-conditioned applicability predicate. Knowledge use is entirely model-mediated; nothing computes applicability.

## Verification, validation & safety

This is the platform's defining weakness and the sharpest contrast with Hacker Bob.

- **No finding verification of any kind.** `record_vulnerability` / `CreateVulnerability` is pure CRUD — title/severity/status/proof written straight to SQLite from the model's (or user's) assertion. No re-execution, no second-opinion verifier, no freeze-then-verify, no severity grader, no claim dedup, no evidence binding. `false_positive` is just a status the model can set.
- **The attack-chain graph is decorative, can hallucinate.** A single post-hoc LLM call over the token-truncated transcript emits nodes/edges JSON with LLM-assigned `risk_score` 0-100; the prompt tells the model to *infer* tree structure and causal edges, so it can assert relationships and vulns that never occurred. Not derived from real tool-execution records.
- **No scope enforcement at the tool boundary.** Grepping `internal/security/*.go` for `blocklist|sandbox|deny|scope` returns nothing. Scope is a prompt-level string only; the executor will scan/attack whatever the LLM names. The `exec` tool runs arbitrary `sh -c` and `execute-python-script`/`install-python-package` exist → effectively unconstrained shell / host RCE by design. Server binds `0.0.0.0` by default.
- **Prompts actively suppress safety reflection.** Every orchestrator/sub-agent opens with an "授权状态" block: never question/debate/verify whether authorized ("authorization has been pre-adjudicated"), combined with a "$500+ bounty / top-0.1% hacker / never give up / 2000+ steps" greed framing. The nested `pent_claude_agent` repeats "已获得全部授权,无需再次质疑授权问题," its config commits a literal `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: "sk-xxx"` placeholder, and it inherits arbitrary `setting_sources` skills.
- **Real controls that DO exist** are operational/access-oriented, not correctness: HITL approval middleware (edit-args / soft-reject-as-tool-result so the model keeps iterating; default whitelist `[read_file, list_dir, glob, grep]`, empty whitelist ⇒ every tool needs approval; orphaned interrupts auto-rejected on restart); per-IP web-API rate limiter (protects the **server**, not targets — no target-side request/WAF budget); per-tool timeout + `allowed_exit_codes`; hashed audit log; Bearer-token auth (auto-generated strong password) + HTTPS self-signed default; vision-bytes-out-of-history hygiene. The deep `orchestrator.md` alone adds a soft refusal list (mass destruction/ransomware/unauthorized intrusion) that coexists with the never-question directive.

Posture summary: **operator-trust + access-control, with genuinely offensive primitives (C2, reverse shell, webshell, host PTY) gated only by the operator's own HITL settings, and zero finding-correctness machinery.**

## Standout strengths (ranked)

1. **Context-engineering middleware stack** — summarization-by-ratio + large-result reduction-to-disk + orphan-tool-pruning (telemetry-consistent) + dynamic tool_search (N resident, rest regex-unlocked). The single most transferable asset; directly targets long-run failure modes.
2. **Live-observability model** — reconnectable `TaskEventBus`-mirrored SSE timeline (refresh/multi-tab re-attach mid-run), fine-grained event taxonomy (main vs sub-agent vs plan-phase, streamed CoT, tool deltas), plus in-loop steering (inline approve/reject/edit-args, cancel-one-tool-with-a-note that re-enters context). Excellent prosumer UX.
3. **Pragmatic Eino reuse** — three swappable orchestration topologies, checkpoint/resume, plan-execute/supervisor prebuilts, transient-retry-as-segment, and a uniform tool bridge "for free" instead of reinventing a loop.
4. **Clean three-layer knowledge separation** (roles=persona+tools, agents=identity+instruction, skills=methodology) with `bind_role` stitching one tool registry across single/multi-agent, and a faithful validated Agent-Skills progressive-disclosure implementation.
5. **Recursive agent composition + multi-role MCP** — agent-as-MCP-tool (`pent_claude_agent`), simultaneous MCP server/host/federator on the official Go SDK, soft-error self-correcting tool protocol.
6. **Record-as-you-go fact/finding blackboard** and the pre-delegation completeness gate — thoughtful answers to context loss and sub-agent amnesia.
7. **Breadth of integration surface** — Burp send-to-agent bridge, mobile chatbots, vision-bytes-out-of-history discipline, broad real coverage across web/network/API/cloud/container/binary/forensics/post-ex.

## Real limitations (ranked, honest)

1. **No verification/grading/dedup layer whatsoever** — findings, severity, and the attack graph are all model self-narration; the single biggest correctness gap.
2. **No scope/target enforcement + unsandboxed shell** — scope is a prompt string; `exec`/python tools = host RCE; offensive C2/persistence/exfil far beyond bounty scope.
3. **Jailbreak-flavored "never question authorization" prompting** plus insecure-by-default offensive endpoints (`0.0.0.0` reverse shell, unauthenticated MCP HTTP default, committed `sk-xxx`).
4. **Over-engineered-vs-content RAG mismatch** — sophisticated indexer/reranker/sub-index machinery over a 2-category corpus that ships disabled, with brute-force O(N) full-table cosine that won't scale, and threshold/silos inconsistencies.
5. **Massive prompt duplication** — high-intensity/bounty/exploitation/tool-failure blocks copy-pasted across all orchestrators + Go functions; drift already visible ("2000+ steps" vs "大量步骤"). Only the blackboard fragment is DRY.
6. **Single hardcoded model for every agent** — no orchestrator/worker tiering; `baseModelCfg` reused.
7. **Static, non-contextual selection** — tools/knowledge exposed per-role, never per-target-surface; skills not bound to roles/agents (discovery purely model-driven); shipped sub-agents under-use `bind_role`.
8. **Overstated scope** — "100+ tools" = 89 recipes; mimikatz listed without a recipe; mobile is a methodology-skill facade (no frida/apktool); AD has no dedicated role/BloodHound integration.
9. **Fragile front end / Eino coupling** — 73K LOC hand-rolled vanilla JS with zero tests and a hand-rolled SVG graph renderer; several files re-implement Eino package-private behavior (upgrade-fragile).
10. **China-network / Chinese-first coupling** — qwen default, Tsinghua/goproxy mirrors, Chinese-only skills/roles/tool-description strings; English is a translation layer. Single squashed "Add files via upload" git commit obscures provenance.

## Maturity (tests/docs/activity/production-readiness)

A polished, actively-maintained (commits through 2026-06-02, versioned `v1.6.31`, `run.sh`/`upgrade.sh` with backup/restore, OTel, Discord/WeChat, 404Starlink + Tencent badges) **product surface with a verification void underneath**. Tests: 50 `_test.go` against 252 Go source files — concentrated exactly on the orchestration runtime that matters (`eino_summarize_test.go`, `orphan_tool_pruner_*`, `plan_execute_*`, `tool_error_*`, `max_iterations_test.go`, `reasoning/eino_test.go`, run-loop edge cases). But the **security-sensitive layers are largely untested**: `internal/skillpackage` (the uploaded-bundle parser/validator with path-traversal surface) has no tests, the roles loader has none, the RAG engine has only 4 (no recall/quality tests), `executor_test.go` covers arg-building not safety, and the two Python MCP servers + the Java Burp extension ship zero tests; multi-agent/einomcp integration tests are an admitted backlog. Docs are excellent and bilingual (42KB README + Chinese twin, per-directory READMEs, fully-commented `config.yaml`, focused docs for multi-agent/robot/i18n/vision). Repo hygiene is weak (~20 committed Java build artifacts). Net: a real prosumer-grade offensive web console — strong engine/UX engineering and documentation, with finding-correctness and trust-posture as deliberate non-goals. (Verified counts: 89 tools, 13 roles, 16 agents, 23 skill dirs, 2 KB categories, 252 Go files / 50 tests, `max_iterations: 12000`, `knowledge.enabled: false`, default provider qwen3-max with Claude bridge.)
