### Profile: poc-lab

I have enough to resolve the count discrepancy (16 vuln dirs: 15 `CVE-*` + 1 `QVD-*`; the analyses' "17/18" over-counted). Writing the consolidated profile.

## Identity (one paragraph: what this project IS, plainly)
`poc-lab` (GitHub `Unclecheng-li/poc-lab`, MIT, a single squashed commit `5372451` dated 2026-06-04) is a **curated corpus of working exploit proof-of-concepts for recently-disclosed, mostly-2026 high-severity CVEs, each paired with an unusually deep, bilingual root-cause write-up.** It is 16 vulnerability directories (15 `CVE-*` + 1 `QVD-*`), one per vuln, each holding a PoC (`exploit.py`/`exp.c`/`exploit.sh`, sometimes an `env/` Docker lab or precompiled `build/` ELF) plus a heavily-templated `README.md`. **It is explicitly NOT an agent, scanner, automation harness, or framework** — it is a static reading/teaching reference. The only AI association is a one-line pointer in the root README to a *separate* repo, VulnClaw ("AI-powered penetration testing framework"); none of that machinery is present here.

## Purpose & intended user
A reference/teaching corpus for security researchers and exploit developers who want to understand recently-disclosed high-severity bugs end-to-end: the root cause anchored to real upstream source and the exact fixing diff, the full exploitation mechanism, reproduction steps with expected output, and tiered mitigations. The audience is humans (the English root README is just an index; all technical depth is Chinese-only), and the value proposition is documentation quality, not tooling.

## Architecture overview (the spine of the system)
There is **no control flow, no data flow, no shared library, and no runtime "system"** — the architecture is purely an organizational convention:
- **Directory-as-record:** each top-level dir = one vuln, named `<CVE-or-QVD-id> <codename>` (e.g. `CVE-2026-43284 Dirty Frag/`). Sub-layout is `exploit/` (always), plus optional `env/` (Docker lab — only NGINX Rift), `build/` (committed ELF binaries — PinTheft, Dirty Frag 43500, SSH Keysign), and evidence (`dmesg.txt`, `demo.mp4`).
- **README-template-as-schema:** every per-CVE README rigidly follows one spine — TL;DR blockquote → 速览/overview table (CVE / codename / type / CVSS vector / CWE / discoverer / patch commit / preconditions) → 受影响范围 (per-distro affected/fixed matrices) → 根因分析 (layer-by-layer root cause with real source snippets + fix diff) → 利用机制 (ASCII attack-chain + PoC walkthrough) → 复现步骤 (env, quick-check bash, compile, run, expected output, exit-code semantics) → 修复方案 (tiered mitigations) → 时间线 → 参考资料 → disclaimer. This is called the "Copy Fail README template"; newer entries note they were "根据 Copy Fail README 模板补全."
The individual PoCs are standalone programs with a trivial internal flow (detect preconditions → build payload → fire → self-check hit/miss). The corpus is README-dominated: C exploits total ~7,000 lines (two Dirty Frag PoCs at 1,951 lines each, Fragnesia 1,302), yet several headline PoCs are deliberately ~10 lines (Copy Fail, Valkey RESP DoS) — the knowledge is in the prose, not the code.

## Agentic design (how/if it uses LLMs/agents: model, loop, planning, memory, tool use, single vs multi-agent)
**None.** There is no LLM, no agent, no planning loop, no memory, no tool use, single- or multi-agent — confirmed by grepping the tree for `agent|orchestrat|claude|gpt|openai|anthropic|langchain|llm|autonomous|pipeline` (only coincidental substring hits like `universal_newlines`). The agentic tooling, if any, lives in the separate VulnClaw repo. The closest thing to a "driven loop" anywhere is inside one PoC artifact — the cPanel `scanner.py` `ThreadPoolExecutor` multi-target scanner — but that is an exploit tool, not framework infrastructure. For a head-to-head against Hacker Bob (a multi-agent, MCP-driven orchestration framework), `poc-lab` is not a comparable system on the automation/agentic axis; it is a knowledge artifact.

## Capability inventory (bulleted, concrete)
- **Sophisticated full-chain exploit primitives, not toy PoCs.** The "Copy Fail" Linux page-cache-write family (its heart) — `splice()` a read-only file's page cache into a crypto/network in-place-decrypt path that writes before auth fails: Copy Fail (AF_ALG `authencesn`), two Dirty Frag variants (xfrm-ESP; RxRPC/rxkad), Fragnesia (ESP-in-TCP AES-GCM keystream byte-control), DirtyDecrypt (RxGK krb5enc). Dirty Frag 43500 (1,951 lines) ports kernel `fcrypt` to userspace to offline-search a session key that decrypts `/etc/passwd` bytes into `root::0:0`, then PTY-bridges `su -` through PAM `nullok`.
- **Other kernel LPE primitives:** Slab Cross-Cache (KFENCE `skb_small_head_cache` mis-free via `BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN`), PinTheft (RDS zerocopy + io_uring `FOLL_PIN` 1024-bias refcount theft + PCP LIFO page grooming + dangling `struct page*`), SSH Keysign (`pidfd_getfd` mm==NULL ptrace bypass FD-theft), CIFSwitch (`cifs.spnego` missing `vet_description` → root via malicious NSS module).
- **Browser:** Chrome `CSSFontFeatureValuesMap` UAF (iterator-invalidation, HTML/JS PoC).
- **C-service / parser bugs (the Bob-relevant subset):** Redis/Valkey `RESTORE` zipmap length-prefix desync (heap OOB read, ASan oracle, ships upstream `dump.tcl` 36-byte vector), NGINX Rift rewrite alloc-vs-copy heap overflow (full Docker lab), Valkey pre-auth RESP assert/abort DoS, HTTP/2 HPACK amplification DoS.
- **Web/app logic:** cPanel2Shell `%2F` validate-before-decode traversal + RAII privilege-drop lifetime bug (with a bundled CVE-2026-41940 auth-bypass detector); Notepad++ config-trust `ShellExecute` command injection.
- **One real offensive tool:** `cPanel2Shell-Scanner/exploit/scanner.py` (687 lines) — multi-target `ThreadPoolExecutor`, side-effect-free auth-bypass detector + active CalDAV traversal chain (TLS-cert-SAN domain harvesting, SMTP plus-alias Maildir planting, `%2F` retry ladder), INI config, JSONL output, meaningful exit codes.
- **Reproducible lab:** NGINX Rift `env/Dockerfile` pinned to nginx commit `98fc3bb78`, ASLR disabled via `setarch x86_64 -R`.

## Knowledge system (how security/vuln knowledge is encoded & retrieved)
Knowledge is encoded as **human-readable prose against a fixed README template — and nothing else.** There is no machine-readable layer: no JSON/YAML knowledge base, no schema, no tags, no index beyond the directory listing, no Semgrep/CodeQL/YARA, no detection signatures, no query corpus. "Detection" exists only as ad-hoc bash one-liners inside mitigation sections. Within that constraint the discipline is genuinely strong:
- **Root cause grounded in real source + the exact fix diff** (e.g. Copy Fail's vulnerable `scatterwalk_map_and_copy(...,out=1)` OOB write vs the fix; Slab Cross-Cache's one-line `kfree(head)` fix; Redis's canonical-length check).
- **Cross-CVE family synthesis:** five CVEs are unified as the "Copy Fail" class with a contrast table (entry path / write location / write unit / target / race-dependent), distilled to one invariant: "optimization passes references around; the security boundary still assumes every layer knows who owns the memory."
- **Implicit, language-portable root-cause patterns** that *do* generalize to source auditing/fuzzing: validate-vs-consume length/canonicalization desync (Redis zipmap, NGINX Rift), validate-before-decode (cPanel `%2F`), error/cleanup path doesn't roll back side effects (Copy Fail, RDS double-free), parser-FSM field not reset on early-return (Valkey `c->reqtype`), MAC-then-decrypt ordering inversion (krb5enc), iterator invalidation (Chrome), missing origin validation on a privileged callback (CIFSwitch confused deputy).
**Retrieval is "a human reads the markdown."** Any tool reuse requires an NLP/LLM distillation pass per entry.

## Verification, validation & safety
No repo-level verification harness (no tests, CI, or assertions for the corpus itself). Validation is per-PoC and ad-hoc, with three recurring patterns: (1) **self-check after the action** — PinTheft `memcmp`s against `SHELL_ELF` before `execl`; Dirty Frag re-mmaps and byte-compares the page cache; (2) **exit-code-as-verdict taxonomy** — Fragnesia documents `1`=vuln verified, `0`=fixed/no-mutation, `2`=local error, `4`=namespace-gate-closed; (3) **sanitizer-as-oracle** — Redis instructs building Valkey with ASan and reading `heap-buffer-overflow in zipmapNext` as confirmation; plus a **differential/patched-vs-unpatched oracle** (root-only `/etc/shadow` returning content vs empty).
**Safety is honor-system only.** Every README carries an authorized/lab-only disclaimer and some advise VM snapshots, synthetic targets, and print restore commands (PinTheft backs up the SUID binary). But the PoCs are genuinely weaponized against real system files (`/usr/bin/su`, `/etc/shadow`, `/etc/sudoers.d/`), CIFSwitch writes a real passwordless-sudo entry, and the repo **ships committed compiled ELF binaries** (`build/exp`, `sshkeysign_pwn`, etc.) with no hashes or reproducible-build manifest — untrusted attacker binaries that must never be run. Notably, the `/etc/shadow`-content oracle and the scanner's `preview: data[:200]` of real secret files are precisely the "harvest real PII/secrets to confirm a finding" anti-pattern Bob's own project rules forbid (`feedback_no_pii_harvest_confirmation`); adopt the differential-oracle *idea*, never the secret-dump execution.

## Standout strengths (ranked)
1. **Best-in-class vuln documentation.** Each README ties a working PoC to real upstream source, the exact fixing commit/diff, CVSS vector + CWE, per-distro matrices, a step-by-step attack chain, and tiered mitigations with copy-paste commands — the gold standard for explaining a CVE end-to-end.
2. **Cross-CVE family synthesis.** Recognizing five bugs as one "read-only page cache enters writable crypto path" class with a contrast table is genuine analytic abstraction, not a flat dump.
3. **Intellectual honesty as a first-class field.** Explicit trigger-PoC-vs-full-exploit labeling, "non-universal" caveats, refusal to fabricate missing version/timeline data ("暂缺公开一手来源"), and documented exit-code semantics make each artifact's reliability legible — closely aligned with Bob's honest-severity-gating ethos.
4. **A small set of language-portable, class-level root-cause heuristics** (length/canonicalization desync, validate-before-decode, error-path-no-rollback, FSM-field-not-reset, MAC-then-decrypt) that transfer to C-source auditing and fuzzing.
5. **Reproducible, authoritative artifacts** for the service bugs — pinned Docker labs and reused upstream test vectors (Redis `dump.tcl`).

## Real limitations (ranked, honest)
1. **Not an agent/tool at all** — no automation, orchestration, or machine-readable layer. On the dimension Hacker Bob competes (autonomous discovery + verification), there is nothing here to compare; it is a static dataset.
2. **~60% out of Bob's operating scope.** ~10–11 of 16 entries are Linux-kernel LPE / binary-exploitation (slab/PCP grooming, `FOLL_PIN` arithmetic, scatterlist internals, ptrace ACLs) plus a Chrome UAF and Notepad++ — these are about *escalating* a known bug to root, not *finding* it (Bob's job), and Bob has no kernel/syscall/QEMU harness.
3. **Zero machine-readable structure** — no schema, index, tags, or detectors; knowledge is locked in free-form, Chinese-only Markdown requiring per-entry distillation.
4. **Largely transcribed, not original.** Several headline PoCs are verbatim rewrites of third-party work (Copy Fail = theori's 10-liner; Redis = upstream test vector); multiple entries openly mark "no primary source."
5. **Untrustworthy committed ELF binaries** — opaque, hash-less supply-chain/safety anti-pattern.
6. **No provenance or iteration history** — single squashed commit, all files dated 2026-06-04.
7. **Inconsistencies:** the `CVE-2026-43494 PinTheft/` folder's own README titles it `QVD-2026-27616`; template completeness varies widely (Copy Fail exhaustive; Dirty Frag 43500/Fragnesia/CIFSwitch riddled with `暂缺` gaps).

## Maturity (tests/docs/activity/production-readiness)
**Documentation maturity: very high. Engineering/process maturity: low.** The READMEs are polished, templated, source-grounded, and internally consistent — clear evidence of significant manual effort and real template discipline. But there are no tests, no CI, no contribution tooling beyond a PR checklist, no per-PoC build reproducibility, no versioning, and a single squashed commit (`.gitignore` is a one-line `.workbuddy/`). It is a well-maintained **content** project, not a maintained **software** project, and as a dataset to wire into a tool it is immature. **Net for the head-to-head:** `poc-lab` is a knowledge/PoC corpus, not a competing framework — what it offers Hacker Bob is borrowable *discipline* (the report-writeup template, family-abstraction tables, honest-gating fields, ~5 service-level C/web root-cause patterns and known-trigger fuzzing seeds), realized best as a one-time distillation, not an integration; there is no agent, orchestration, or automation to adopt, and most exploit content is out of scope and unsafe to run.

---

## Head-to-head comparisons (full)


### Comparison: raptor

I have verified enough across both codebases. Writing the comparison.

## Honest verdict

These are **serious peers in different rooms of the same house** — both are mature, Claude-Code-native security frameworks, but they barely overlap in what they actually do. RAPTOR is a heavyweight **source/binary/supply-chain analysis engine** (300K non-test LOC, 916 `test_*.py`, real Z3/SMT, CodeQL/Coccinelle/Semgrep, AFL++/libFuzzer, DWARF+nm reachability, SCA/SBOM/VEX) whose agentic layer is bolted onto a fundamentally **scanner-driven, deterministic pipeline**; its strategic "agency" is real only inside bounded sub-tasks, and its live-web side is a confirmed stub (`/web` empty, `SecOpsAgentKit` an uninitialized submodule). Bob is a **portable MCP-owned, event-sourced exploration engine** that genuinely does live-web bug-bounty and 6-chain smart-contract auditing — domains where RAPTOR is non-functional — but its OSS/native engine is comparatively thin (token-contract-only static scan, EVM-only symbolic via Halmos, no reachability/severity stamping on `main`, no general SAST). Neither is "behind" the other; each is clearly ahead in its own center of gravity and clearly weaker in the other's. RAPTOR is the more rigorously *engineered verification-and-refutation machine*; Bob is the more *strategically exploratory and operationally portable* framework with stronger live-target safety. The single most consequential asymmetry: RAPTOR spends serious effort *not* calling the LLM (free deterministic refutation, SMT unsat pruning, dead-code suppression, statistically-gated cheap-tier prefilter), while Bob feeds essentially everything to LLM evaluators and trusts LLM judgment for grade quality.

## Category note

**Only partially the same problem.** Both are "point an agent swarm at a target, get defensible findings with reproducible evidence," and both are Claude-Code frameworks with slash commands, sub-agents, lifecycle hooks, and convention/CI-enforced metadata. But the target classes diverge almost completely: RAPTOR's center is **C/C++/native memory-corruption exploitability, OSS forensics, and supply-chain risk** on local code/binaries/archives; Bob's center is **live authenticated web targets and on-chain smart contracts**, with OSS source audit as a third mode ("Plane O"). The only true head-to-head surface is **OSS source auditing** — and there RAPTOR is materially deeper on the static/native/verification axis while Bob is more exploratory and better at orchestration/state. They are best read as complementary engines, not competitors.

## Where raptor genuinely beats Bob

- **Pre-LLM deterministic refutation economics** — RAPTOR runs free gates (IRIS absence-of-flow, SMT `unsat`, fixture/reachability suppression) that *kill or downgrade candidates before any paid LLM call*, with savings counters. `core/llm/scorecard/prefilter.py`, `packages/llm_analysis/prefilter.py`, `libexec/raptor-smt-validate-path`. **Why it matters:** Bob has no pre-LLM refutation — every surface costs wave/LLM budget regardless of provable reachability; this is the core cost+precision lever Bob lacks.

- **SMT/Z3 path-feasibility with C semantics** — drops unreachable paths and feeds *concrete* trigger inputs into prompts; models real C arithmetic (`umul_overflows` for CWE-190, width coercion, shift disambiguation). `core/smt_solver/{csem.py,rejection.py,witness.py}`, `libexec/raptor-smt-check-*`. **Why it matters:** makes "unreachable/infeasible" *provable* rather than an LLM guess; Bob's only symbolic engine is Halmos, EVM-only.

- **Binary-oracle reachability suppression** — DWARF+nm classify functions `symbol_present/inlined/folded/absent`; `absent ∧ ¬inlined ⇒ dead code` is hard-suppressed pre-LLM with a `suppressions.jsonl` audit trail. `core/binary/`, `libexec/raptor-binary-oracle-{e2e,precision}`. **Why it matters:** this is exactly the reachability/severity-ceiling capability Bob is missing on `main` (see gaps below).

- **Statistically-honest self-calibration** — Wilson-95%-bound cheap-tier FP prefilter, per-`(model, decision_class)` trust cells, held-out generalization numbers for binary-oracle suppression. `core/llm/scorecard/{scorecard.py,prefilter.py,judge.py}`. **Why it matters:** RAPTOR measures its own reliability and only short-circuits under a statistical bound; Bob's grade *quality* is fully LLM-trusted (only the arithmetic is server-checked).

- **Prompt-injection / hostile-content defense for the agent itself** — per-call nonce envelope, datamarking (Hines et al.), slot discipline, role placement, per-model defense profiles, plus Landlock+seccomp+mount-ns / Seatbelt sandbox. `core/security/prompt_envelope.py`, `core/sandbox/{landlock.py,seccomp.py,mount_ns.py,seatbelt.py}`. **Why it matters:** RAPTOR treats target source, scanner output, and prior LLM output as adversarial *inputs to its own agents*; Bob redacts secrets in outputs but does not quarantine untrusted bytes flowing into agent prompts.

- **x-source provenance anti-hallucination guard (loop-level)** — tool-input fields tagged `"x-source":"discovered"` are blocked unless the value appeared in the prompt or a prior successful tool output. `core/llm/tool_use/loop.py` (lines ~443-462, `_get_discovered_fields`). **Why it matters:** stops fabricated identifiers/values *at call time*; Bob catches fabricated evidence only after the fact via verify-time evidence re-derivation.

- **Self-growing knowledge from confirmed outcomes** — Tier-3 verified-outcome exemplar retrieval primes prompts with RAPTOR's *own* prior oracle-confirmed bugs; KNighter-style checker synthesis turns one confirmed bug into a variant-hunting rule. `core/verified_outcome/exemplars.py`, `packages/checker_synthesis/`. **Why it matters:** the corpus sharpens as it runs; Bob's friction loop only widens *tool availability*, never technique quality.

- **CWE-strategy YAML playbooks with a scoring picker** — 8 bug-class playbooks (`signals`/`key_questions`/`prompt_addendum`/CVE `exemplars`), picker pins +100 on CWE-id match, injected *outside the data zone*. `core/llm/cwe_strategies/strategies/*.yml`, `picker.py`. **Why it matters:** structured, testable, CWE-routed guidance; richer than Bob's ~11-entry substring-scored technique registry + 8 bypass tables.

- **Three-engine static + SCA/SBOM/VEX supply-chain arc** — Semgrep→CodeQL→Coccinelle source-intel (allocation/privilege/abort-dominance idioms) plus OSV/KEV/EPSS/SSVC enrichment, CycloneDX SBOM, VEX, self-calibrating risk model. `engine/coccinelle/source_intel/`, `packages/sca/`, `core/cve/`. **Why it matters:** Bob enumerates dependency surfaces but does no vuln-DB enrichment and has no structural C intel or SARIF dedup pipeline.

- **Headless CI/automation mode** — `python3 raptor.py scan` emits SARIF/SBOM/VEX with no Claude Code at all. **Why it matters:** RAPTOR runs unattended in pipelines; Bob is two-turn human-gated by design and cannot.

## Where Bob beats raptor

- **Live-web is real vs. a stub** — Bob has scope-enforced HTTP (SSRF v4/v6 blocklist, DNS-pin, per-redirect re-validation), a Patchright stealth browser with OAuth/SPA record-replay, auth-differential BAC/IDOR, and doc-delta (OpenAPI-3/GraphQL-SDL/Postman). RAPTOR's `/web` is an alpha stub with empty SSRF rules and an uninitialized offsec kit. For live bounty work, RAPTOR is non-functional out of the box.

- **Smart-contract auditing across 6 chain families** — EVM/SVM/Aptos/Sui/Substrate/CosmWasm with live typed RPC reads and fork-pinned PoC runners (Foundry concrete + Halmos symbolic + Anchor/Move/cargo). RAPTOR has none of this.

- **Genuinely exploratory, strategy-forming orchestration** — MCP-owned event-sourced frontier + wave/graph schedulers + hypothesis/transition nodes mean the *engine* decides where to look and pivots on what it learns. RAPTOR's macro flow is **scanner-driven** (verified: targets are scanner candidates reordered by `--prefer` globs in `raptor_agentic.py`; no agent hypothesizes *where* to look). This is Bob's clearest architectural advantage.

- **MCP-owned, content-addressed state authority** — hash-bound, lock-serialized, crash-recoverable, fully replayable session state with a single dispatch funnel and role-bundle separation of duties. RAPTOR's durable state is JSON/JSONL governed by convention with many `except Exception: pass` best-effort blocks and no owned state-authority server.

- **Live-target operational safety + PII guardrails** — scope re-checked on every call/redirect, temp-email tool, hard rule against submitting operator PII, audit-graded immutable report/grade paths. RAPTOR's safety is excellent for *self-defense against hostile code* but it has no live-target scope/PII model because it doesn't touch live targets.

- **Portability** — one neutral source fans out to Claude/Codex/Kimi/generic-MCP adapters with `--check` drift gates. RAPTOR is a single Claude-Code framework.

## Bob's real gaps exposed by raptor

- **No reachability/severity-ceiling stamping on `main`** — confirmed: `attack_vector`/`severity_ceiling`/`network_reachable` are whitelisted *consumers* in `assignment-brief.js` (lines 138-145) and `wave-brief-derivation.js`, the comment says "Stamped onto OSS native-code surfaces by repo-target.js," but **nothing in `mcp/lib` actually writes these fields** (grep for any assignment returns empty; `dashboard.js:188` reads a `reachability.max_credible_severity_ceiling` that nothing populates). RAPTOR's binary-oracle + IRIS *is* this capability, shipped. **Why it matters:** this is the operator-acknowledged dominant limit — Bob reliably lands MED/LOW on its own. **Severity: HIGH.**

- **No pre-LLM deterministic refutation / cost gating** — Bob feeds all bucketed surfaces to LLM evaluators; there is no "provably unreachable/infeasible → skip the wave spend" path and no cost-savings accounting. RAPTOR's free gates + Wilson-bound prefilter are absent. **Why it matters:** both precision (fewer LLM false leads) and economics (token/$ per run). **Severity: MED-HIGH.**

- **No general symbolic/SMT feasibility outside EVM** — Halmos covers EVM only; OSS/native and non-EVM chains have no path-feasibility or concrete-witness extraction. **Why it matters:** "unreachable" stays an LLM guess; no concrete trigger inputs fed to evaluators. **Severity: MED.**

- **No prompt-injection envelope / untrusted-content quarantine into agent prompts** — Bob redacts secrets (`redaction.js`, `sensitive-material.js`) but does not nonce-envelope/datamark untrusted HTTP bodies, OSS source slices, or ingested audit reports before they reach agents (grep for inject/datamark/spotlight in `mcp/lib` finds only vuln-*detection* code). **Why it matters:** an agent processing hostile target content is itself an attack surface; this is squarely in Bob's threat space (it ingests attacker-controlled responses). **Severity: MED.**

- **No record-time provenance guard on claim/evidence inputs** — Bob's evidence completeness is re-derived from artifacts at verify time (good), but nothing blocks an agent from submitting a fabricated value via `record-candidate-claim.js` that never appeared in a real tool output. RAPTOR's x-source guard does this at the loop. **Why it matters:** fail-fast anti-hallucination vs. catch-it-later. **Severity: MED.**

- **Grade quality is fully LLM-trusted; no self-calibration** — only the grade arithmetic/thresholds are server-validated; there is no FP-rate measurement, no held-out generalization numbers, no statistically-gated short-circuit. **Why it matters:** Bob can't quantify or bound its own reliability. **Severity: MED.**

- **No knowledge that learns technique quality** — confirmed: no checker synthesis, no verified-outcome exemplar retrieval; the friction loop only widens tool availability. RAPTOR's corpus sharpens from its own confirmed bugs. **Why it matters:** Bob's small static substring-scored corpus doesn't improve with use. **Severity: MED.**

- **Thin native/OSS static engine** — `bob_static_scan` is token-contract only; Bob can shell `semgrep` via the OSS docker sandbox (it's a CLI-tool-pack seed, `cli-tool-packs.js:220`) but has no CodeQL/Coccinelle integration (CodeQL is explicitly "Deferred" in that file), no SARIF dedup/routing, and no dependency vuln-DB enrichment (OSV/KEV/EPSS) despite enumerating dependency surfaces in `repo-target.js`. **Why it matters:** OSS mode is the one head-to-head domain and Bob is mechanically weaker there. **Severity: MED.**

## Adaptation candidates

**raptor-1 — Reachability + severity-ceiling stamping for OSS native surfaces (fixes the dominant limit).**
- Source feature: binary-oracle reachability suppression (`core/binary/`, `libexec/raptor-binary-oracle-*`) + IRIS absence-of-flow refutation (`packages/llm_analysis/codeql_packs/.../CallableInventory.ql`).
- Adopt: a free static pass that classifies native functions (nm/DWARF dead-code; absence-of-dataflow for shelled CodeQL/Semgrep) and **stamps the already-consumed `attack_vector`/`severity_ceiling`/`network_reachable` fields** so the brief/grade can lift findings above MEDIUM (or suppress dead ones) *before* spending a wave.
- Effort: **M-L** (the brief consumers already exist; the producer is the missing half).
- Value: **HIGH** (directly closes Bob's #1 acknowledged limit).
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/repo-target.js` (stamp at surface-creation), new `mcp/lib/reachability.js`, consumed by `assignment-brief.js`/`wave-brief-derivation.js` (already wired) and the wave planner.

**raptor-2 — Pre-LLM refutation gate + cost-savings accounting in the wave planner.**
- Source feature: free deterministic gates + savings counters (`core/llm/scorecard/prefilter.py`, `packages/llm_analysis/prefilter.py`).
- Adopt: before spawning evaluators, run cheap deterministic checks (reachability from raptor-1, SMT unsat from raptor-3, dependency not-in-vuln-DB) that demote/skip surfaces and log "LLM calls avoided."
- Effort: **M**.
- Value: **HIGH** (precision + token/$ economics; ties to the existing benchmark plan).
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/assignments.js` / wave bucket planner; record to `tool-telemetry`/`pipeline-analytics.js`.

**raptor-3 — General SMT/Z3 path-feasibility MCP tool (beyond Halmos/EVM).**
- Source feature: `core/smt_solver/{csem.py,rejection.py}`, `libexec/raptor-smt-validate-path` (sat/unsat + concrete witness, C-semantics overflow predicates).
- Adopt: a `bob_smt_check` tool that takes path conditions (from OSS evaluators or chain-builder hypotheses), returns sat/unsat + concrete trigger, prunes infeasible hypotheses and feeds witnesses into briefs.
- Effort: **L** (new Z3 dependency, sandboxed runner).
- Value: **MED-HIGH**.
- Integration point: new `mcp/lib/smt-runner.js` + `mcp/lib/tools/smt-check.js`; consumed by OSS evaluator and `chain-state-tree.js`.

**raptor-4 — Prompt-injection envelope for untrusted content in briefs.**
- Source feature: `core/security/prompt_envelope.py` (per-call nonce, datamarking, slot discipline, role placement, per-model profiles).
- Adopt: wrap untrusted bytes (HTTP response bodies, OSS source slices, ingested audit-report/schema text) in a nonce+datamark envelope before they enter assignment briefs.
- Effort: **M**.
- Value: **MED-HIGH** (agent self-security in Bob's actual threat space).
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/assignment-brief.js` + `mcp/lib/body-resolvers/` + `http-records.js`/`audit-report-parser.js`.

**raptor-5 — Record-time x-source provenance guard on claims/evidence.**
- Source feature: `core/llm/tool_use/loop.py` x-source `"discovered"` validation.
- Adopt: when an agent calls `bob_record_candidate_claim`/evidence write, validate that key proof values (IDs, tokens, response snippets) appeared in a prior session tool output (http-audit/source); reject fabricated values at call time.
- Effort: **M**.
- Value: **MED** (complements existing verify-time evidence re-derivation with a fail-fast gate).
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/tools/record-candidate-claim.js`, `mcp/lib/evidence.js`, cross-checked against `http-records.js`.

**raptor-6 — Cheap-tier FP prefilter with Wilson-bound trust before the 3-round verify.**
- Source feature: `core/llm/scorecard/` (Wilson-95% prefilter, per-`(model, decision_class)` trust cells).
- Adopt: a cheap-model "is this a confident false positive?" prefilter that short-circuits clearly-bad candidate claims before freeze/verify, activating only once its measured miss-rate upper bound is ≤ a threshold.
- Effort: **L**.
- Value: **MED** (cost + first measured self-reliability signal for Bob).
- Integration point: new `mcp/lib/scorecard.js`; gate in `mcp/lib/claim-freeze.js` / `verification.js`.

**raptor-7 — Reasoning-divergence (semantic-entropy) trigger in the verification adjudicator.**
- Source feature: `core/llm/semantic_entropy.py` (Jaccard token-set divergence over reasoning).
- Adopt: in the deterministic adjudicator, when brutalist and balanced *agree on verdict but cite different sinks/identifiers*, raise low-confidence and force the final round / re-replay — a trigger Bob's adjudicator currently lacks (it fires on disagreement/low-confidence, not agree-but-diverge).
- Effort: **S-M** (pure function, dependency-free).
- Value: **MED**.
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/verification.js` / `bob_build_verification_adjudication`.

**raptor-8 — Verified-outcome exemplar retrieval (self-growing corpus).**
- Source feature: `core/verified_outcome/exemplars.py` (Tier-3: prime prompts with own prior confirmed findings).
- Adopt: feed Bob's own SUBMIT-graded, verification-confirmed findings back into evaluator briefs as calibration exemplars for matching surface types, read across sessions from the grade-verdict store.
- Effort: **M**.
- Value: **MED** (turns Bob's static knowledge into a corpus that sharpens with use).
- Integration point: new `mcp/lib/verified-outcome-exemplars.js` reading `grade-verdict-store.js`; injected via `assignment-brief.js`.

**raptor-9 — Offline dependency vuln-DB enrichment for OSS dependency surfaces.**
- Source feature: `core/cve/{osv,kev,epss}` + `packages/sca/`.
- Adopt: enrich the dependency frontier events Bob already emits (`repo-target.js:818`) with offline/paste-fed OSV/KEV/EPSS matches to prioritize known-vulnerable dependencies — reusing Bob's existing paste-only, no-fetch pattern (`cve-feed-parser.js`/`cve-scope-matcher.js`).
- Effort: **M**.
- Value: **MED** (cheap, high-signal OSS surface ranking; respects the no-network rule).
- Integration point: `mcp/lib/repo-target.js` dependency events + extend `mcp/lib/cve-scope-matcher.js`.

### Comparison: CyberStrikeAI

I have thorough verification of both sides. Writing the comparison now.

---

## Honest verdict

They are **different categories that overlap only at "an LLM drives security tooling,"** so neither is simply "ahead" — but on the axis they share, they optimize opposite ends and each is genuinely strong there. CyberStrikeAI (CSAI) is a polished, operator-driven **red-team console** with excellent runtime engineering (Eino-based context-compaction middleware, a reconnectable live SSE run-timeline, in-loop HITL steering, full kill-chain breadth incl. C2/webshell/reverse-shell) and a **deliberate verification void** — `record_vulnerability` is pure SQLite CRUD (`internal/app/vulnerability_tools.go:254`), the attack-chain graph is a single post-hoc LLM call with model-assigned risk scores (`internal/attackchain/builder.go:85,258`), and there is **zero scope enforcement** at the tool boundary (no blocklist/sandbox/scope hits anywhere in `internal/security/`; `executor.go` shells out via `sh -c`). Hacker Bob is the inverse: a single-operator, human-gated **scoped-evaluation engine** whose entire reason for being is the machinery CSAI lacks — freeze→3-round-adversarial-verify→code-adjudicated→grade, content-addressed event sourcing, SSRF/scope hardening, sandboxed runners, and a much richer record-as-you-go state model. CSAI is more *usable and observable in real time* and spans post-exploitation; Bob is far more *trustworthy, defensible, and safe*, with real multi-chain and OSS-audit engines CSAI has no analog for. CSAI is a **serious peer in runtime/UX engineering and a different-category tool in mission**, not behind Bob and not ahead of it — it is what Bob would look like if you deleted the correctness layer and bolted on a server and a C2. The single sharpest contrast remains finding-correctness: CSAI trusts the model's self-narration end-to-end; Bob never trusts the model for verdict math.

## Category note

**Mostly different problems.** Bob targets **authorized, scoped bug-bounty / SC-audit / OSS-CVE disclosure** — its non-negotiables are scope-confinement, finding verification, severity grading, PII guardrails, and triage-ready reports. CSAI targets **full-engagement red-teaming you control** — its non-negotiables are kill-chain coverage (recon → exploit → C2 → lateral → exfil → cleanup), operator UX, and getting out of the model's way ("授权已由系统前置裁决…不得质疑" — never question authorization, `orchestrator_instruction.go:20-24`). They share only the substrate: "wrap CLI security tools + a knowledge corpus behind an LLM agent loop and an MCP surface." On that shared substrate the comparison is fair; on mission it is apples-to-oranges. CSAI explicitly makes scope-enforcement and finding-correctness **non-goals**, which would be disqualifying for Bob's use case and are by-design for CSAI's.

## Where CyberStrikeAI genuinely beats Bob

- **Live, reconnectable in-run observability** — `TaskEventBus` mirrors every SSE frame so a refreshed page or a second tab re-attaches to a *running* task mid-stream, with a fine event taxonomy (main vs sub-agent vs plan-phase, streamed CoT, tool deltas). Evidence: `internal/handler/agent.go:139,766-767,924`. Why it matters: Bob's only run-time visibility is a **manual-Refresh, post-hoc cross-session analytics page** (`mcp/lib/dashboard.js` — `http` server on 127.0.0.1:4873 rendering KPI tables with a `<button id="refresh">`); there is no live stream of an in-flight hunt. Watching a long autonomous run as it happens is a real operator need Bob doesn't serve.

- **In-loop HITL steering at tool granularity** — approve / soft-reject-as-tool-result (so the model keeps iterating) / edit-args / cancel-one-tool-with-a-note that re-enters context, via a tool interceptor middleware. Evidence: `internal/multiagent/hitl_middleware.go` (`HITLToolInterceptor`, `NewHumanRejectError`). Why it matters: Bob's human gating is **coarse** — a two-turn handshake at *wave* boundaries plus `bob_set_operator_note`; an operator cannot intercept, edit, or veto an individual tool call mid-wave.

- **Runtime context-compaction middleware for long single-agent loops** — summarization-by-ratio (trigger 0.8, `eino_summarize.go`), orphan-tool-message pruning that keeps `tool_call ↔ tool_result` pairing valid for OpenAI-compatible APIs (`orphan_tool_pruner_middleware.go`), and dynamic `tool_search` (only ~12 tools resident, rest regex-unlocked) — all with unit tests (`eino_summarize_test.go`, `orphan_tool_pruner_middleware_test.go`). Why it matters: Bob *sidesteps* long-context drift by construction (short-lived stateless agents + bounded briefs + `resume`), but for any genuinely long single-conversation loop CSAI's machinery is more directly applicable and battle-tested.

- **Full kill-chain breadth incl. post-exploitation** — a built-in Go C2 framework (`internal/c2/`, 19 files: tcp/http/https/websocket listeners, AES-256-GCM templated beacon), plus standalone `reverse_shell` and `pent_claude_agent` MCP servers and an AntSword-class webshell + host PTY. Why it matters: Bob deliberately has none of this (out of scope for disclosure) — so CSAI "wins" only if your mission is red-team ops, but it is a real capability axis Bob does not occupy.

- **Swappable orchestration topologies + checkpoint/resume "for free"** — four runtime-selectable engines over one definition set (`eino_single` / `deep` / `plan_execute` / `supervisor`) via Eino ADK, with checkpoint/resume and transient-retry segmentation. Evidence: `internal/multiagent/eino_orchestration.go`, `eino_checkpoint.go`, `plan_execute_*`. Why it matters: Bob has exactly one (deterministic wave + graph scheduler) — that determinism is a feature for auditability, but CSAI gets exploration-topology flexibility and crash-resume without hand-rolling it.

- **Broad integration surface into existing operator workflows** — Burp "send to agent" bridge, DingTalk/Lark/WeChat robots, and a 17-page web SPA. Why it matters: Bob is Claude-Code-only; it cannot be driven from Burp or a chat client.

- **Generic third-party MCP federation** — CSAI is simultaneously an MCP server, host, and federator with `name::tool` namespacing + health/call-count monitoring (`internal/einomcp`, `internal/mcp`). Why it matters: Bob is an MCP server only; it can attach exactly one curated external MCP (`@brutalist/mcp` roast) but has **no generic mechanism to federate arbitrary external MCP tool servers** into a hunt.

## Where Bob beats CyberStrikeAI

- **Finding verification — the decisive gap.** Bob has freeze → 3-round adversarial verify (`brutalist/balanced/final`) → **code-computed adjudication** → 5-axis grade with server-validated arithmetic (`mcp/lib/verification.js`, `claim-freeze.js`, `verification-round-store.js`, `build-verification-adjudication`, `grade-verdict-store.js`). CSAI has **none of it**: `record_vulnerability` writes the model's assertion straight to SQLite (`vulnerability_tools.go:240-272`), `false_positive` is just a status the model can set, and the attack-chain graph can hallucinate edges/vulns (single LLM call over a truncated transcript, `attackchain/builder.go`).

- **Scope enforcement + SSRF hardening.** Bob re-checks scope on every session-bound call and every redirect hop, with an IPv4/IPv6 SSRF blocklist and DNS-pinning (`bob_http_scan`). CSAI has **zero** target-boundary enforcement — scope is a prompt string; `exec`/python tools are unconstrained host RCE; server binds `0.0.0.0` by default.

- **Sandboxed execution.** Bob's SC PoC runners and OSS runner use flag allowlists, env scrubbing, `--network none`, `--cap-drop ALL`, read-only non-root containers. CSAI's `executor.go` is a thin `exec.CommandContext(... "sh","-c", ...)` wrapper with per-tool timeouts only.

- **Real multi-chain SC auditing + OSS fuzzing engines** — 6 chain families with live typed RPC reads + fork-pinned PoCs (Foundry/Halmos/Anchor/Move/cargo) and Docker-sandboxed OSS build/fuzz/repro. CSAI has no smart-contract or source-audit engine at all.

- **Registry-driven single-source-of-truth with `--check` drift gates** and content-addressed event sourcing (`frontier-events.jsonl` → deterministic materializers; full hash lineage). CSAI has pervasive **prompt duplication** across orchestrators/Go functions (visible drift: "2000+ steps" vs "大量步骤") and a single squashed git commit obscuring provenance.

- **Safety posture.** Bob has a hard operator-PII guardrail (temp-email tool) and audit-graded immutable paths. CSAI's prompts **actively suppress** safety reflection ("never question authorization" + "$500+/top-0.1%/never give up" framing, `orchestrator_instruction.go:72-80`) and ship a committed `sk-xxx` placeholder.

- **Correctness-path test coverage** — ~141 test files including policy-replay (authority), drift-coherence, and hook write-guard tests. CSAI's 50 tests concentrate on the *orchestration runtime*; its security-sensitive layers (`skillpackage` bundle parser, roles loader, RAG quality, executor *safety*, both Python MCP servers, Burp extension) are largely untested.

## Bob's real gaps exposed by CyberStrikeAI

- **No live in-run observability stream — severity: medium.** Bob can show cross-session analytics but cannot stream an in-flight hunt's events. For long autonomous waves an operator is blind until settlement. This matters more as run duration grows.

- **Coarse human-in-the-loop granularity — severity: medium.** Steering is wave-boundary + operator-note only; there is no per-tool-call approve/edit/veto. A wrong-but-not-yet-merged action can't be caught mid-flight (CSAI catches it at the tool interceptor).

- **No generic external-MCP-tool federation — severity: low/medium.** Bob's tool universe is its own ~148 frozen tools plus one hard-wired roast server. It cannot pull a third-party MCP tool server into a hunt the way CSAI's host layer does. Limits extensibility without a code change.

- **Browser-screenshot context hygiene — severity: low.** Bob returns screenshots into the agent's multimodal context directly; CSAI deliberately keeps image *bytes* out of agent history and injects only text summaries (`analyze_image`). For screenshot-heavy SPA hunts Bob spends more context on raw images. (Note: Bob *already* applies exactly this summary-at-append/body-on-pull discipline to every *other* artifact type via `mcp/lib/body-resolvers/` + `bob_resolve_body` — screenshots are the one body type not routed through it.)

- **Severity ceiling (pre-existing, not newly exposed) — severity: high but already known.** Confirmed on `main`: `elevated_severity` does not exist; `severity_ceiling` is only a hardcoded default `40` (`wave-brief-derivation.js:133`, `assignment-brief.js:140`) with no reachability producer merged. CSAI doesn't fix this either (its severity is pure model assertion), so it exposes the gap by contrast but offers no real remedy.

**Analogs Bob already has (NOT gaps):** large-result reduction → `body-resolvers/` + `bob_resolve_body` (typed, scope-checked, 1 MB-paginated — stronger than CSAI's spill-to-disk); record-as-you-go fact/finding blackboard → `bob_log_dead_ends`/`log_coverage`/`log_technique_attempt`/`record_candidate_claim`/`log_capability_friction` (richer than CSAI's 2-bucket blackboard); soft-error self-correcting tool protocol → typed envelope with closed `ERROR_CODES`; pre-delegation completeness gate → `mcp/lib/assignment-brief.js` (bounded, surface-scoped); progressive-disclosure skills → `capability-playbooks.js` (read-on-demand); recursive agent composition → wave/graph subagent spawn; runtime summarization middleware → bounded-by-construction stateless agents + `resume` (different design, mostly N/A). CSAI's RAG is **not** a model to copy — sophisticated indexer over a 2-category corpus shipped `knowledge.enabled: false` (`config.yaml:211`) with brute-force O(N) cosine.

## Adaptation candidates

**CyberStrikeAI-1 — Live event-tail run dashboard (SSE).**
Source: `TaskEventBus` reconnectable SSE timeline (`handler/agent.go:139,766`).
Adopt: add an SSE/long-poll endpoint to Bob's *existing* `mcp/lib/dashboard.js` HTTP server that tails the already-on-disk append-only `frontier-events.jsonl` (and verification-round/wave-handoff writes) for the active session, so an operator watches surfaces/claims/waves stream live with refresh-reattach. Bob already has both the server and an event ledger, so this is mostly plumbing, not new state.
Effort: **S/M**. Value: **high**. Integration point: `mcp/lib/dashboard.js` + `mcp/lib/session-events.js` + the `frontier-events.jsonl` ledger.

**CyberStrikeAI-2 — Screenshot bytes-out-of-context discipline.**
Source: CSAI `analyze_image` keeps image bytes out of agent history, injecting text summaries only.
Adopt: route `bob_browser_take_screenshot` outputs through the same body-on-pull pattern Bob already uses for other artifacts — return a distilled text summary + artifact-ref by default, resolve raw image bytes only via an explicit `bob_resolve_body` pull. Closes the one body type not already routed this way.
Effort: **S**. Value: **medium**. Integration point: `mcp/lib/tools/` browser tools + add a `screenshot` resolver under `mcp/lib/body-resolvers/`.

**CyberStrikeAI-3 — Finer-grained in-wave operator steering.**
Source: HITL interceptor with edit-args / soft-reject-as-tool-result / cancel-with-note (`hitl_middleware.go`).
Adopt: extend `bob_set_operator_note` into a structured "mid-wave directive" (per-surface veto / re-prioritize / inject-hint) that the next `bob_start_next_wave` merge consumes deterministically — getting most of CSAI's steering value without a persistent server loop (which Bob's Claude-Code-skill architecture can't host). Honest scope: this enriches Bob's *existing* operator-note + wave-merge path; it cannot intercept a single live tool call.
Effort: **M**. Value: **medium**. Integration point: `bob_set_operator_note` / operator-note store + wave scheduler (`bob_start_next_wave`, `apply_wave_merge`).

**CyberStrikeAI-4 — Reasoning-effort → provider-field abstraction.**
Source: `internal/reasoning/eino.go` maps a single "effort" intent to provider-specific fields (Claude budget thinking, DeepSeek `thinking`, OpenAI `reasoning_effort`).
Adopt: codify Bob's currently-memory-driven model/effort routing (Opus-first, Sonnet-fallback, balanced-verifier on Opus) into a small declarative routing table consumed by the spawn layer and fanned out by the existing `generate-*` scripts, so routing is registry-driven like everything else rather than living in operator memory.
Effort: **S/M**. Value: **low/medium**. Integration point: verifier configs / role-bundle metadata + `scripts/generate-agent-tools.js`.

**CyberStrikeAI-5 — Dynamic tool resident-set for the non-Claude adapters.**
Source: CSAI `tool_search` (≈12 tools resident, rest regex-unlocked on demand) (`eino_middleware.go:163-220`).
Adopt: for Bob's Codex/Kimi/generic adapters (which lack Claude Code's built-in deferred-tool/ToolSearch loading), add an optional on-demand tool-unlock shim so those hosts don't pay full ~148-tool schema cost up front. Explicitly **not needed under Claude Code**, which already defers tool schemas — so value is adapter-only.
Effort: **M**. Value: **low**. Integration point: `adapters/` (Codex/Kimi) + `scripts/generate-*` tool-surface generation.

---

### Comparison: poc-lab

## Honest verdict (4-7 sentences)

They are not peers, and the comparison is asymmetric by category, not by quality. `poc-lab` is a 16-directory, single-squashed-commit (`5372451`, 2026-06-04, MIT) human-curated corpus of working exploit PoCs with exceptionally deep bilingual root-cause write-ups; it has zero agent, orchestration, automation, or machine-readable layer (confirmed: the only "AI" is a one-line link to a *separate* repo, VulnClaw). Hacker Bob is an event-sourced, MCP-owned, multi-agent autonomous-discovery-and-verification *framework* (~173 lib modules, ~148 tools, freeze→3-round-verify→grade→chain engine, 6-chain SC auditing, Docker OSS fuzzing). On the axis Bob competes — finding bugs and producing defensible, verified reports — poc-lab is not a competitor; it does no finding, no verification, no automation. The honest framing: poc-lab is a best-in-class *knowledge artifact* and Bob is a *system*; poc-lab beats Bob only on documentation craft, cross-CVE family abstraction, and as a donor of concrete parser/service root-cause patterns and seed vectors, and roughly 60% of its content (kernel LPE, browser UAF, binary pwn) is entirely outside Bob's operating scope. There is no world where poc-lab "outperforms" Bob as a tool, and no world where Bob's prose write-ups match poc-lab's per-CVE depth.

## Category note (are they even solving the same problem?)

No. Bob solves *autonomous discovery + adversarial verification + triage-ready reporting* for authorized engagements. poc-lab solves *teaching a human exactly how a specific disclosed bug works and how to weaponize it* — it is a reference dataset organized as directory-per-vuln with a rigid README template ("Copy Fail README template"). They overlap only at one thin seam: both care about root-cause clarity for C-service/parser bugs (Redis RESTORE, NGINX Rift, Valkey RESP, HPACK), which is the slice of poc-lab inside Bob's OSS-audit scope. Everything else in poc-lab (Linux page-cache-write crypto family, slab cross-cache, `FOLL_PIN` refcount theft, `pidfd_getfd` ACL bypass, Chrome UAF, Notepad++) is post-discovery *escalation-to-root* exploit engineering, which Bob has no harness for and does not attempt.

## Where poc-lab genuinely beats Bob

- **End-to-end exploitation depth (proves HIGH/CRITICAL impact)** — full weaponized chains, not triggers: PinTheft (`exp.c`, 786 lines + committed `build/exp` ELF) chains RDS zerocopy + io_uring `FOLL_PIN` refcount bias + PCP LIFO page grooming; Dirty Frag 43500 (`exp.c`, 1,951 lines) ports kernel `fcrypt` to userspace to recover a session key and PTY-bridges `su -`. Why it matters: poc-lab's deliverable *demonstrates root*, whereas Bob's deliverable stops at "reachable bug + sanitizer repro" and structurally cannot show the escalation that justifies CRITICAL.

- **Cross-CVE family synthesis** — five CVEs (Copy Fail, two Dirty Frag, Fragnesia, DirtyDecrypt) unified as one "read-only page cache enters writable crypto path" class with a contrast table (entry path / write location / write unit / target / race-dependent) and a single distilled invariant. Evidence: `CVE-2026-31431 Copy Fail/README.md`. Why it matters: this is genuine analytic abstraction; Bob's knowledge is a flat per-surface taxonomy with no class-level family grouping.

- **Disclosure-grade documentation** — every README (500–778 lines) ties a working PoC to real upstream source, the exact fix commit/diff, an explicit CVSS-4.0 vector string, CWE, per-distro affected/fixed matrices, a mermaid/ASCII attack chain, a timeline, references, and tiered mitigations. Evidence: `CVE-2026-25243 .../README.md` (CVSS 4.0 7.7 vector, GHSA-c8h9-259x-jff4, Valkey fix `fea0b4064`, mermaid `D-->E-->F-->G`). Why it matters: Bob's `reporter.md` per-finding template is intentionally triage-minimal (Title/Severity/CWE/Endpoint/PoC/Evidence/Impact/Remediation, "omit methodology") — lighter than poc-lab on CVSS vectors, version matrices, fix-commit references, and timelines.

- **Reusable known-trigger seed vectors** — ships upstream `dump.tcl`-derived 36-byte zipmap RESTORE vector, the NGINX Rift pinned Docker lab (`env/Dockerfile` at nginx `98fc3bb78`, ASLR off via `setarch -R`), and concrete RESP/HPACK malformed frames. Why it matters: these are drop-in fuzz/repro seeds for exactly Bob's OSS `fuzz_run`/`taint_trace` surfaces, and Bob's corpus has no curated cross-project seed library.

- **A real offensive tool inside one PoC** — `CVE-2026-29205 cPanel2Shell-Scanner/exploit/scanner.py` (687 lines): `ThreadPoolExecutor` multi-target, side-effect-free auth-bypass detector + active CalDAV `%2F` validate-before-decode traversal ladder, TLS-SAN domain harvesting, INI config, JSONL output, exit-code verdicts. Why it matters: it is a clean reference for a focused single-bug-class scanner, a shape Bob doesn't ship.

## Where Bob beats poc-lab

- **It is an actual system.** Autonomous surface discovery → wave scheduling → freeze → 3-round adversarial verification with code-computed adjudication → 5-axis grade → chain-building. poc-lab has no control flow, no runtime, no verification harness for its own corpus (no tests/CI/assertions).
- **Verification rigor.** Independent brutalist/balanced rounds merged by a deterministic adjudicator with full hash lineage; poc-lab's validation is per-PoC, ad-hoc (self-`memcmp`, exit-code taxonomy, ASan oracle) and honor-system.
- **Safety architecture.** SSRF/scope hardening, DNS-pinning, `--network none --cap-drop ALL --read-only` Docker sandbox, audit-graded immutable paths, PII/temp-email guardrails. poc-lab ships untrusted committed ELFs (`build/exp`, `sshkeysign_pwn`) with no hashes and weaponizes real system files (`/etc/shadow`, `/usr/bin/su`).
- **Breadth.** Web + 6 smart-contract families + OSS source audit through one pipeline; poc-lab is a static reading list.
- **Machine-readable, reproducible state.** Event-sourced JSONL, registry-driven tools, `--check` drift gates, crash-recovery. poc-lab is single-squashed-commit, Chinese-only free-form Markdown with zero schema, index, or provenance.

## Bob's real gaps exposed by poc-lab

- **Severity-ceiling vs. demonstrated-impact gap — severity: HIGH.** Verified on `main`: `severity_ceiling`/`attack_vector`/`network_reachable` exist only as *consumer* fields (`wave-brief-derivation.js:130-135`, `assignment-brief.js`); nothing stamps them (`repo-target.js` does not produce them), there is no `elevated_severity` field anywhere, and chains add at most +15 grade points (`grader.md:10`). poc-lab's whole deliverable is the escalation evidence Bob can't produce, so Bob reliably lands MED/LOW where poc-lab demonstrates HIGH/CRITICAL. Honest caveat: this gap is only *partially* actionable — closing it for kernel/binary classes would require harnesses (QEMU/syscall/grooming) that are out of Bob's scope and that poc-lab does by hand, not by any technique Bob could mechanically adopt.

- **No curated class-level root-cause family knowledge for parser/service bugs — severity: MED.** Bob's OSS bug taxonomy lives as an inline prompt list (`evaluator.md:64`) plus a thin substring technique registry (`evaluator-techniques.json`, 13 entries, ~17 KB) and 8 web bypass tables. It has no *cross-project family abstraction* (length/canonicalization desync, validate-before-decode, error-path-no-rollback, FSM-field-not-reset-on-early-return, MAC-then-decrypt) of the kind poc-lab distills. These patterns are language-portable and directly improve C-source auditing yield.

- **Disclosure/CVE report template is thinner than poc-lab's for the OSS path — severity: LOW-MED.** Bob's report finding fields (`reporter.md:36-43`) omit an explicit CVSS-vector string, per-distro affected/fixed matrix, fix-commit reference field, and timeline. For bug-bounty triage this is a deliberate, correct choice ("omit methodology"); but Bob's OSS mode actually files GHSAs/CVEs, where these fields are expected — so it's a real gap *specifically for the disclosure path*, not for web bounty.

- **No formalized patched-vs-unpatched differential oracle in verification — severity: LOW-MED.** Bob gates HIGH native findings on a real non-dry-run repro (`evaluator.md:64`), but doesn't formalize "build the fixed commit, show the repro no longer fires / ASan no longer trips" as a verification step. poc-lab uses differential oracles (patched returns empty vs unpatched returns content; ASan fires only pre-fix). This would raise OSS finding confidence and false-positive resistance.

**Explicitly NOT gaps (Bob already has analogs):** incomplete-fix/residual hunting that reads the actual patch and tests the unfixed sibling (`evaluator.md:66`, `residual_hunt_targets[]`); sanitizer-as-oracle via ASAN/fuzz in the hardened Docker sandbox (`evaluator.md:59,64`); the memory-safety bug taxonomy (`evaluator.md:64`); honest severity-gating, preconditions, and false-positive notes in findings (memory `feedback_oss_severity_honest_gating`); pinned reproducible Docker labs; CWE mapping (extensive, `reporter.md:49-67`); mutate-and-replay differential for web IDOR/CSRF (`evaluator.md:52`).

## Adaptation candidates

**poc-lab-1 — Class-level root-cause family knowledge pack**
Source feature: poc-lab's cross-CVE family contrast tables + distilled invariants (`CVE-2026-31431 Copy Fail/README.md` family table; the validate-vs-consume desync in Redis/NGINX).
Adopt: add a curated `oss_root_cause_families` knowledge entry encoding ~5 language-portable parser/service patterns (length/canonicalization desync, validate-before-decode, error-path-no-rollback, FSM-field-not-reset-on-early-return, MAC-then-decrypt), each with a detection hint and a worked witness — so they get scored/selected into OSS assignment briefs, not just buried in a prompt list.
Effort: M. Value: med-high.
Integration point: `.hacker-bob/knowledge/evaluator-techniques.json` + `mcp/lib/technique-packs.js` (`scoreTechniqueEntry`), surfaced into the OSS evaluator brief via `bob_select_technique_packs`.

**poc-lab-2 — Curated cross-project fuzz/repro seed vectors**
Source feature: reused upstream test vectors and pinned labs (Redis `dump.tcl` 36-byte zipmap vector; NGINX Rift `env/Dockerfile`@`98fc3bb78`; RESP/HPACK malformed frames).
Adopt: a small `oss-seed-vectors/` knowledge dir of known-trigger malformed inputs keyed by surface/protocol, referenced by `fuzz_run` recipes so OSS fuzzing seeds from a real-vuln corpus instead of only repo `testdata`.
Effort: S-M. Value: med.
Integration point: OSS `fuzz_run` lens in `prompts/roles/evaluator.md` + `bob_repo_docker_run` `recommended_commands[]`; store under `.hacker-bob/knowledge/`.

**poc-lab-3 — Disclosure-grade report template for the OSS/CVE path**
Source feature: the per-CVE README schema (CVSS-vector string, per-distro affected/fixed matrix, fix-commit reference, timeline, references, mermaid attack chain).
Adopt: add optional disclosure-mode sections to the composed report for `oss_*` capability packs — `cvss_vector`, `affected_fixed_matrix`, `fix_commit`, `timeline`, `references` — so GHSA/CVE submissions carry expected metadata. Keep the lean triage template for web bounty.
Effort: S-M. Value: med.
Integration point: `bob_compose_report` section shapes (`mcp/lib/tools/compose-report.js`) + `prompts/roles/reporter.md` (OSS branch only).

**poc-lab-4 — Patched-vs-unpatched differential repro oracle**
Source feature: differential oracles (patched returns empty / ASan fires only pre-fix; exit-code-as-verdict taxonomy).
Adopt: for OSS HIGH native findings, add an optional verifier replay step that builds the fixing commit and re-runs the repro, recording "fires on vuln commit, clean on fixed commit" as an evidence row — raising confidence and FP-resistance. Adopt the differential *idea only*; never the secret-dump execution (violates Bob's PII rule, memory `feedback_no_pii_harvest_confirmation`).
Effort: M. Value: med-high.
Integration point: brutalist/balanced verifier replay + OSS evidence pack (`bob_write_evidence_packs`), driven through `bob_repo_docker_run`.

**poc-lab-5 — Structured exploitation-completeness label**
Source feature: poc-lab's explicit "trigger-PoC vs full-exploit" and "non-universal/preconditions" honesty fields + documented exit-code semantics.
Adopt: a small structured `exploitation_completeness` enum on findings (`trigger_only` / `partial_chain` / `full_exploit`) feeding the grader's severity-accuracy axis, making the trigger-vs-weaponized distinction machine-legible rather than prose-only.
Effort: S. Value: low-med.
Integration point: `bob_record_candidate_claim` schema + `grader.md` severity-accuracy axis. Note: Bob already enforces honest severity-gating and preconditions/false-positive notes, so this is a small structuring delta, not a new capability.
